name: Whispering Farms Equestrian Center
description: A full-service equestrian facility in the Dallas/Fort Worth area offering exceptional boarding and training with a focus on horse welfare and rider development. Staff is on premises 24/7, providing premium Tifton 85 hay four times daily and top-quality grain. Three in-house trainers plus an assistant trainer host clinics and special events throughout the year.

trainers.name: Whispering Farms Training Team
trainers.bio: Three in-house trainers specializing in dressage, eventing, and hunter/jumper disciplines.

name: Meadow Lake Equestrian Center
description: A picturesque 130-acre full-care boarding and show facility in Lancaster, Kentucky. With 45 roomy stalls across 3 barns, spacious paddocks with traditional 4-board fencing, and multiple arenas including a large well-lit indoor, Meadow Lake offers top-quality care in a quiet atmosphere. The facility hosts Mini Trials, Combined Tests, and USEF/USDF Dressage Classics, and features the Ashley Inn Bed & Breakfast with 6 guest rooms.

trainers.name: Meadow Lake Coaching Staff
trainers.bio: Multiple on-site coaches providing lessons and training in eventing and dressage at all levels.

name: North Jersey Equestrian Center
description: The Tri-State's premiere riding center featuring New Jersey's largest climate-controlled indoor arena at 135x300 feet. With 103 matted 12x12 stalls, trainers specializing in Western, English, Hunters, Jumpers, and Dressage, and seven-day-a-week operation from 8am to 8pm. Horses receive three daily feedings and turnout seven days weekly.

trainers.name: NJEC Training Staff
trainers.bio: Experienced trainers covering Western, English, Hunters, Jumpers, and Dressage at all levels.

name: Riviere Equestrian Farm
description: A world-class equestrian facility inaugurated in March 2022, located just 7 miles from the World Equestrian Center and HITS Ocala. Features 22 brand new rubber-matted stalls, a covered arena (220x120 ft), large paddocks with daily turnout, and an A/C tack room. Offers full board, dry stalls, and specialized retirement board with individualized care for retired horses.

trainers.name: Riviere Farm Staff
trainers.bio: Experienced equestrian professionals providing top-quality care and training near the World Equestrian Center.

name: Eden Farms
description: A family-owned horse stable on 160 acres of rolling fields in Upstate South Carolina near Greenville. Offers 40 stalls, horseback riding lessons, trail rides through the Blue Ridge Mountains, and hosts birthday parties and special events. Home to the Happy Hooves Therapeutic Equestrian Center, serving special needs children and adults through equine-assisted programs.

trainers.name: Eden Farms Staff
trainers.bio: Experienced staff providing riding instruction, trail guiding, and therapeutic equestrian programs.

name: Castlewood Ranch
description: A 30-acre private equestrian facility in the Aptos Hills of California. Offers limited horse boarding in covered pipe stalls, large shared paddocks, or seasonal pasture. Features a 60-foot redwood round pen with sanded and rubberized footing, a 100x120 arena with adjustable and fixed jumps, a cross-country course with 10 field-type jumps, and a hot/cold wash station with two covered groom areas.

trainers.name: Castlewood Ranch Instructor
trainers.bio: Experienced instructor offering individual and group lessons with a focus on jumping and cross-country training.

name: James River Equestrian Center
description: A well-established boarding and training facility in Richmond, Virginia featuring 22 spacious 12x12 stalls, two dusk-to-dawn lighted arenas, and abundant turnout space. Stall boarders receive a mandatory weekly lesson to ensure rider-horse partnership development. Field boarding is also available for horses that thrive in an outdoor setting.

trainers.name: JREC Instructor
trainers.bio: Experienced riding instructor providing mandatory weekly lessons for stall boarders and optional training programs.

name: Garrod Farms Stable
description: A unique equestrian facility paired with an estate winery in the heart of Silicon Valley. Set in Saratoga, California, Garrod Farms offers boarding options ranging from fields and pastures to paddocks and barn stalls. Special services include custom feeding programs, horse trailer parking, and tack boxes. A great option for Bay Area equestrians seeking a scenic, nature-rich boarding experience.

name: Greene Valley Stables
description: A 38-acre horse boarding facility in Ruckersville, Virginia offering both indoor and outdoor boarding. Features a 20-stall barn, a 60x200 lighted show-regulation indoor riding arena, and a wash stall with hot and cold water. Private lessons available at affordable rates, making it a great choice for riders at all levels in the Central Virginia region.

trainers.name: Greene Valley Instructor
trainers.bio: Private riding instructor offering lessons at $40/hour across multiple disciplines.

name: Keelona Farm
description: Established in 1984, Keelona Farm sits on 75 acres with miles of open riding trails in Charlottesville, Virginia. The custom-built 14-stall barn features 12x12 stalls with several 12x24 foaling stalls. A well-respected facility known for decades of quality horse care in the heart of Virginia's horse country.

name: Sunny Fox Farms
description: An equestrian facility in the Texas Hill Country serving San Marcos and Wimberley. Boarding includes access to tack room space, use of all amenities, deworming, supplement and blanket management, and three free lessons per week. A great value option for riders looking for full-service care with included instruction.

trainers.name: Sunny Fox Farms Instructor
trainers.bio: On-site instructor providing three included lessons per week for all boarders.

name: Riverbend Equestrian Center
description: A premier boarding facility in Jupiter Farms, Florida featuring 12x12 stalls, an on-site barn manager, a 266x135 ft fully irrigated ring, and individual grass turnouts. Caters to hunters, jumpers, dressage, and trail riders. The barn includes an air-conditioned lounge with full-size refrigerator, ice maker, TV, couch, microwave, and WiFi throughout.

trainers.name: Riverbend Training Staff
trainers.bio: Experienced trainers specializing in hunters, jumpers, and dressage with on-site barn management.

name: Westridge Farms
description: Premier Hunter/Jumper stable located in Dripping Springs, Texas, only 30 minutes west of downtown Austin. Westridge Farms welcomes riders of all levels, offering quality training, exceptional care, and a fun atmosphere. The team attends 'A' rated shows and local clubs both regionally and nationally, and offers lessons, training, and horse sales/leases.

trainers.name: Christine Rishell
trainers.bio: Owner and head trainer. Started riding at age 5, earned USEF Bronze Medal in 2012. Trains with enthusiasm, creativity, structure, respect, and humor.

trainers.name: Mia Kertson
trainers.bio: Assistant trainer and Texas Tech graduate with a successful junior and collegiate riding career.

name: Redwood Farm
description: Where Excellence Meets Horsemanship. Redwood Farm works with riders of all levels to transform them into top competitors through a comprehensive program built on horsemanship, trust, and partnership between horse and rider. The facility specializes in importing and developing quality horses while providing personalized training. Riders have been guided to USEF Pony Finals, USEF Medal Finals, ASPCA Maclay Finals, and more.

trainers.name: Ashleigh Stewart
trainers.bio: Founder and head trainer with 18+ years of experience. Grew up riding with Fran and Joe Dotoli in Massachusetts. Detail-oriented, hands-on instruction emphasizing thoughtful development and long-term success.

trainers.name: Hayden Stewart
trainers.bio: Co-trainer who finished Top 25 at both the USEF Medal Finals and the ASPCA Maclay Finals in 2016. Competed in High Junior Jumpers and national grand prix. Texas A&M Equestrian Team rider.

trainers.name: Debbie Seymour
trainers.bio: Freelance trainer with 35+ years in the hunter/jumper industry. Co-founded Bit by Bit Farms in 2002. Has coached riders to Pony Finals and Junior Hunter Finals.

name: Rio Vista Farm
description: The Leading Hunter Jumper Barn in Austin, Texas since 1995. Rio Vista Farm is a full care Hunter Jumper Boarding and Training Facility offering private and group lessons for beginners through competitive riders, summer camps for ages 7-16, and a show team competing from Cross Rails to Grand Prix. Riders regularly appear in year-end high-point standings at National, State, and Local levels.

trainers.name: Susan McMarris
trainers.bio: Head trainer. Meredith Manor School of Equestrian Studies graduate (1984). Operating hunter/jumper barns since 1985. Board Member of the Texas Hunter Jumper Association. Specializes in taking riders from summer camp level to year-end high point winner.

trainers.name: Emilie Gardner
trainers.bio: Assistant trainer and West Texas A&M graduate who competes nationally.

trainers.name: Lauri Bothern
trainers.bio: Assistant trainer with 20+ years of teaching experience, working with all age groups.

trainers.name: Haylee Mynyk
trainers.bio: Assistant trainer with 25 years of experience, present since Rio Vista's inception.

name: Glendaloch Farm
description: Glendaloch Farm focuses on the joy of riding. Students are taught horsemanship and Hunter/Jumper riding skills that can take them to any level. The operation values camaraderie, challenge, and delight in riding and showing, with instruction available for beginners through advanced riders. Led by Crista Bandini with over 40 years of riding experience.

trainers.name: Crista Bandini
trainers.bio: Head trainer with over 40 years of riding experience, offering lessons for hunter/jumper enthusiasts at all skill levels.

name: Polo Club Equine
description: Polo Club Equine is located on 28 acres in the beautiful Hill Country towards Dripping Springs. In each of their barns, you will find clean and spacious stalls. The facility offers equestrian horse training and is open 7 days a week. Rated 5.0 stars on Google.

name: Galloping Springs Ranch
description: Dressage training, lessons, and sales in Dripping Springs. World class instruction in a warm and welcoming environment. Galloping Springs Ranch has operated in the Texas Hill Country for more than 20 years. Home of Ashley Shaw Dressage.

trainers.name: Ashley Shaw
trainers.bio: Dressage specialist providing world class instruction at Galloping Springs Ranch.

name: White Fences Equestrian Center
description: Premium full-care horse boarding and professional training just outside Austin in Manor, Texas. White Fences provides exceptional care, large barns, expansive pastures, and a quiet, supportive environment. Specializing in Dressage with a focused, progressive training program designed to develop balance, connection, and harmony. Also provides personalized horseback riding lessons for youth and adults.

name: Switch Willo Stables
description: Unsurpassed excellence in Hunter/Jumper training and competition. Located on 26 beautifully wooded acres in the heart of Northwest Austin, Switch Willo Stables provides the perfect environment in which horses and riders can develop and flourish. Offers lessons, boarding, training, camps, and horse shows.

trainers.name: Holly Mortimer
trainers.bio: Lessons coordinator at Switch Willo Stables.

trainers.name: Wendy Kratzer
trainers.bio: Camp coordinator at Switch Willo Stables.

name: Scissortail Hill Equestrian
description: A horse riding school in Austin, Texas rated 5.0 stars on Google. Located on US-183, Scissortail Hill Equestrian provides equestrian instruction and training services to the Austin area.

name: Upland Farm
description: Elite dressage training facility for the avid dressage rider in the heart of Texas Hill Country. Founded by Marcia Haas and Ashley Bearden, Upland Farm is a community of good people who love their horses and support each other. Offers stall board with oversized stalls and seasonal turnout, customizable training programs, and horse sales and consignment with connections throughout the US and Europe.

trainers.name: Marcia Haas
trainers.bio: Co-founder of Upland Farm, providing elite dressage training in the Texas Hill Country.

trainers.name: Ashley Bearden
trainers.bio: Co-founder of Upland Farm, focused on developing riders and horses through classical dressage.

name: Cadence Ranch
description: Classical Dressage Training, Instruction, and Sales on 50 acres in Central Texas with 24/7 pasture turnout. Features a standard 20x60 indoor dressage arena, air-conditioned tack room, TheraPlate equine therapy system, and SI PEMF therapy blanket. Customized feeding and training programs developed collaboratively with veterinarians, farriers, and body workers.

trainers.name: Christine Calao
trainers.bio: Owner and head trainer, USDF Gold Medalist.

trainers.name: Emma Winter
trainers.bio: Assistant trainer, USDF Bronze Medalist.

name: Waterloo Dressage
description: Dressage instruction and horse training facility in Austin, Texas operated by Grace Harris. Offers rider instruction, horse training, and horses for sale or lease.

trainers.name: Grace Harris
trainers.bio: Dressage instructor and horse trainer at Waterloo Dressage.

name: Riding Club of Austin
description: A woman-owned full-service equestrian facility conveniently located in Southwest Austin. Offers boarding services and horseback riding instruction. Rated 5.0 stars on Google.

name: Creekside Eventing
description: Creekside Eventing is a 22-acre equestrian facility in Georgetown, TX specializing in 3-day eventing, which includes dressage, show jumping, and cross-country. We provide premium horse boarding with lessons and training from an experienced Olympic-level eventing trainer. Haul-in lessons are welcome when scheduled in advance. Horses for sale available.

trainers.name: Megan Hammerlind
trainers.bio: Olympic-level three-day eventing trainer and co-owner of Creekside Eventing. Specializes in producing competitive eventing horses and riders.

name: Maverick Horseback Riding
description: Maverick Horseback Riding serves central Texas specializing in unique equine experiences. Services include trail rides, private lessons, camps, training, and events across multiple locations including Austin, Lockhart, Bastrop, Blanco, Canyon Lake, Spicewood, and Gonzales.

trainers.name: Maverick Instructors
trainers.bio: Experienced western riding instructors offering private lessons and trail rides across central Texas.

name: Lone Star Ranch
description: Austin's destination for horseback riding, riding lessons, ranch parties, kids' camps, and so much more. Located on a 400-acre ranch just minutes from downtown Austin. Offers both Western and English riding lessons in a lighted and covered arena, as well as summer and holiday horse camps for riders of all ages.

trainers.name: Lone Star Ranch Instructors
trainers.bio: Experienced cowgirls, guys, lesson instructors, and excursion guides offering western and English lessons on a 400-acre Austin ranch.

name: SunCrest Farm
description: SunCrest Farm is located on 18 acres just outside of Austin in Manor, TX. The ideal place for lessons, summer day camps, trail rides, birthday parties, and more. First-time and experienced riders of all ages and disciplines are welcome, offering both western and English riding.

trainers.name: SunCrest Farm Instructors
trainers.bio: Welcoming instructors offering western and English lessons, trail rides, and camps for all ages on an 18-acre property near Austin.

name: Tri-Star Farm
description: At Tri-Star Farm, the mission is to bring the love of horses into the hearts of both new and developing riders. Located on Spicewood Springs Road in Austin, the farm offers riding lessons and boarding for all levels, weekly trail rides, summer programs, and birthday parties.

trainers.name: Tri-Star Farm Instructors
trainers.bio: Dedicated instructors offering riding lessons and training for all levels, with a focus on building a love of horses in new and developing riders.

name: ATX Polo
description: ATX Polo IS polo in Austin! Players of all ages and skill levels get together each week to play the game they love. Welcomes new players and spectators alike. Located at Victory Cheval in Manor, TX. For lessons and chukkers contact Javier Insua.

trainers.name: Javier Insua
trainers.bio: Head instructor at ATX Polo offering lessons and chukkers for players of all ages and skill levels.

name: Oak Bend Farm Polo Club
description: Oak Bend Farm is the home of polo in Central Texas, located one hour from both Austin and College Station and 90 minutes from Houston. Offers seasonal polo memberships, USPA tournaments, a Polo School, horse boarding, hay and horse sales, and vacation rentals including a Barn Loft and Clubhouse.

trainers.name: Oak Bend Farm Polo School
trainers.bio: Certified polo instructors teaching rules, game strategy, riding for polo, and swing mechanics to enable players to participate in club chukkers.

name: Augusta Pines Farm
description: Augusta Pines Farm is a premier full-service 33-acre show barn in Spring, TX bordering The Woodlands, specializing in Hunters, Jumpers, and Equitation.

name: Bailey Oaks Farms
description: The largest English equestrian riding and training center south of downtown Houston in Alvin, TX. Offers premier horseback riding lessons, camps, boarding, and training with a focus on hunter and jumper disciplines.

name: Bedoya Training Stables
description: Houston area's premier hunter/jumper training facility for those looking to compete on the 'A' circuit and beyond. Daniel Bedoya is a veteran of both the World Cup and Olympics.

trainers.name: Daniel Bedoya
trainers.bio: Olympic and World Cup veteran offering premier hunter/jumper training at the 'A' circuit level and beyond.

name: Blackjack Lane Equestrian
description: Competitive English full-service riding lesson, boarding and training barn in Southeast Houston specializing in Hunter, Jumper, Equitation and Dressage for beginners through advanced riders.

name: BlissWood Ranch
description: A 350-acre bed and breakfast dude ranch just one hour west of Houston in Cat Spring, TX. Offers guided trail rides, winery rides, picnics, ranch activities, and unique lodging experiences in the Texas countryside.

name: Blue Fox Farms
description: A dressage facility located at 14801 Old Chocolate Bayou Rd in Houston, TX.

name: Callegari Equestrian Center
description: A premier equestrian facility in Cypress, TX offering boarding, training and riding instruction. Prides itself on being more of a club than an average boarding facility, with a close-knit community of riders.

name: Cypress Trails Ranch
description: Equine riding and training facility in Humble, TX offering gorgeous countryside trail rides, horse boarding with pasture living, parties, and events.

name: Haras dos Cavaleiros
description: Elite Lusitano horse breeding, training, sales and equestrian experiences in dressage, located in Magnolia, TX just north of Houston. Training center open to outside horses.

name: Houston Oaks Equestrian Club
description: The Equestrian Club at Houston Oaks consists of 500 acres of pristine pastures in Hockley, TX with exceptional opportunities for boarding, lessons, training, and horseback riding.

name: Houston Polo Club
description: The largest polo club in the United States, founded in Houston, TX. Located at 8552 Memorial Drive, the club promotes the sport of polo and is private but open to the public on Sundays.

name: Magic Moments Stable
description: Full-service boarding and training facility in West Houston near the Katy Freeway and Wilcrest. Horseback riding school with a focus on Dressage and Western Dressage from beginner to nationally competitive levels.

name: Pine Hollow Farm
description: A unique horse-friendly facility on 30 quiet, lush acres in New Caney, TX for prime Hunter/Jumper show preparation and casual enjoyment.

name: Ride the Wind Stables
description: A western horse riding and boarding facility in Rosenberg, TX on 27 acres, 5 miles southwest of Sugar Land. Offering western lessons and boarding in a relaxed ranch setting.

name: S4 Farm
description: S4 Farm proudly offers hunter-jumper lessons and full training at their Harris County equestrian facility in Hockley, TX. Program designed for every rider.

name: Sobral Performance Horses
description: Located north of Houston near The Woodlands in Montgomery, TX. Specializes in western horse riding lessons and horse training including reining.

name: Sundance Horse Ranch
description: An actual working cattle ranch in Manvel, TX offering over 100 acres of relaxing, tree-lined trail riding through the beautiful Texas landscape.

name: WA Farm
description: WA Farm is the ultimate full-service show barn for Hunters and Jumpers in Tomball, TX. Boards and trains horses and ponies from Walk/Trot through Grand Prix.

name: West Stables
description: Located in Cypress, TX off Highway 290. Offers full and partial horse boarding with stall and pasture options. Riding lessons and trails also available.

name: Brookshire Polo Club
description: Brookshire Polo Club is an active polo club located in Brookshire, TX, approximately 35 miles west of Houston. Home to the Texas Arena League and USPA-sanctioned tournaments including the Keleen & Carlton Beal Cup.

name: 4Hearts Ranch
description: Warm equestrian-friendly boarding facility in Fort Worth featuring indoor and outdoor arenas, trails, and spacious stalls. Offering boarding, lessons, and training.

name: Benbrook Stables
description: Benbrook Stables offers trail rides, horseback riding lessons, and summer camps near Fort Worth. A great family-friendly equestrian destination.

name: Black Star Sport Horses
description: Black Star Sport Horses specializes in dressage training and boarding in Waxahachie, Texas. Offering quality sport horse education at all levels.

name: Brawley Farms
description: Brawley Farms is a premier hunter/jumper facility in Double Oak, Texas offering top-level training, boarding, and showing for horses and riders of all levels.

name: Dallas Equestrian Center
description: Dallas Equestrian Center provides boarding, lessons, and training for dressage, hunter/jumper, and western disciplines in the heart of Dallas.

name: Dallas Polo Club
description: The Dallas Polo Club hosts regular polo matches and offers polo lessons for players of all skill levels near Dallas, Texas.

name: Flower Mound Farm
description: Flower Mound Farm is a hunter/jumper and eventing facility in Flower Mound, Texas offering quality training, boarding, and competition preparation.

name: Fort Worth Stockyard Stables
description: Fort Worth Stockyard Stables offers western trail rides through the historic Fort Worth Stockyards, providing a true Texas cowboy experience.

name: Whistlejacket Farm
description: Whistlejacket Farm is a premier eventing and sport horse facility near Dallas-Fort Worth offering training, boarding, and competition prep for eventers and jumpers.

name: Comly Sport Horses
description: Comly Sport Horses specializes in hunter/jumper and eventing training in Wills Point, Texas with boarding and professional coaching services.

name: Hillcrest Farm
description: Hillcrest Farm in Argyle, Texas is a full-service dressage and hunter/jumper facility offering top-quality boarding, training, and lessons.

name: Marshall Creek Ranch
description: Marshall Creek Ranch in Southlake offers trail rides and western horsemanship in a scenic Texas landscape, perfect for beginners and experienced riders alike.

name: North Texas Equestrian Center
description: North Texas Equestrian Center in Wylie is a premier English and western riding facility offering lessons, boarding, and training for all disciplines.

name: Prestonwood Polo Club
description: Prestonwood Polo Club in Oak Point, Texas offers polo lessons, clinics, and matches in a beautiful North Texas setting.

name: RNR Stables
description: RNR Stables in Corinth, Texas provides full equestrian services including boarding, lessons, and training for western, trail, and hunter/jumper riders.

name: Rocking J Stables
description: Rocking J Stables in Garland offers a full range of equestrian services for DFW riders including western riding, hunter/jumper training, and trail riding.

name: Stockyards Stables
description: Stockyards Stables at the historic Fort Worth Stockyards offers authentic western trail rides through the iconic stockyards district.

name: Three Oaks Equestrian
description: Three Oaks Equestrian in North Richland Hills offers dressage and hunter/jumper training with quality boarding facilities in the DFW area.

name: Touch of Chrome Paints
description: Touch of Chrome Paints in Crowley, Texas offers western horseback riding lessons, trail rides, and quality Paint horse services.

name: Willow Bend Polo Club
description: Willow Bend Polo Club in Little Elm, Texas offers polo lessons, matches, and memberships in a premier North Texas polo environment.

name: Spring Creek Stables
description: Spring Creek Stables offers quality, well-trained lesson horses and full-care boarding in San Antonio. With daily on-site staff, riders can haul in their own horse for lessons or use the arena for just $25/hour. A friendly, professional facility located in the scenic northwest San Antonio area.

name: Oak Valley Stables
description: Oak Valley Stables is a highly-rated equestrian facility in San Antonio offering horseback riding lessons and boarding. Known for kind, patient, and skilled instructors, the stable provides a welcoming environment for riders of all experience levels.

name: Turkey Creek Stables
description: Established in 1988, Turkey Creek Stables is a family-run equestrian facility cultivating a safe and enjoyable environment for the budding equestrian. The facility emphasizes education, safety, and community atmosphere with an unpretentious approach to learning, offering riding lessons, boarding, horsemanship courses, and horse training.

name: Oakwell Farms
description: Oakwell Farms is San Antonio's stable for the competitive rider seeking "A" rated competitions for the Hunters, Jumpers and Equitation. Operating over 10 acres with scenic woodland views, this family business brings over 60 years of experience and marked 40 years in San Antonio in 2024.

name: Wrenwood Stables
description: Nestled at the base of the stunning Texas Hill Country, Wrenwood Farms and Stables offers a peaceful and quiet equestrian facility known for excellence in care, nutrition, breeding, sales and training. Owner Rene' Riddle brings over 40 years of horse care experience with certifications in Equine Nutrition, Equine Biomechanics, and Horsemanship Training, and has earned multiple US National Championship titles.

trainers.name: Rene' Riddle
trainers.bio: Owner/Operator with over 40 years of horse care experience. Certified in Equine Nutrition, Equine Biomechanics, and Horsemanship Training. Multiple US National and international championship titles.

name: Double Diamond Equestrian Center
description: Double Diamond Equestrian Center is a paradise for horses nestled in the Texas Hill Country near San Antonio. Spanning 180+ acres featuring mature trees, manicured grounds, and miles of pipe fencing for pastures, paddocks, and pens. The facility offers world-class equestrian amenities and award-winning trainers.

name: Diamond C Stables
description: For over 25 years, Diamond C Stables & Ranch has been a trusted choice for high-quality, affordable horse boarding and training in a family-friendly environment in the Texas Hill Country near San Antonio. The facility features 12'x12' indoor stalls with automatic fly control, hot/cold wash rack, tack rooms, tie-rack areas, and fans in stalls.

trainers.name: Debbie Culver
trainers.bio: Owner of Diamond C Stables & Ranch with over 25 years of experience in horse boarding and training.

name: Blueberry Creek Stables
description: Home of Hungerford Performance Horses, Blueberry Creek Stables offers lessons and training services for riders of all skill levels with a focus on horsemanship fundamentals. The facility provides group and private lessons in English and western riding, jumping, and drill team, with experienced instructors emphasizing safety and skill development.

name: Driftwood Acres Barn
description: A family-friendly equestrian facility in the Texas Hill Country offering boarding, lessons, and recreational riding. The establishment aims to provide a first class facility with a laid back atmosphere where people can just unwind and enjoy their horses. Situated on 40 acres with 24/7 on-property caretakers.

name: Doringcourt Horse Boarding Stables
description: Family-oriented horse boarding facility established 2004, situated in the Texas Hill Country with a serene environment for equine enthusiasts. Located in Bulverde between FM 1863 and Hwy 46 on Smithson Valley Road, approximately 10 miles north of San Antonio. Features two lighted round pens, a lighted arena, scenic trails, and a certified farrier on-site weekly.

name: Bel Canto Farms
description: Premier equestrian facility offering full-service horse boarding, training, and lessons in the beautiful Hill Country of Texas near Wimberley. The operation emphasizes quality training steeped in the traditions of true horsemanship and sport. Staff includes highly qualified trainers with over 80 years of combined teaching and showing experience. Home of the Texas Equestrian Team and IEA affiliated.

name: Hunters Chase Farms
description: A horseback riding school situated in the Texas Hill Country near Wimberley offering instruction from beginner to advanced levels. The facility emphasizes leadership, teamwork, and discipline through equestrian training. Features two outdoor arenas, a mini cross-country course, bridle paths, two barns with stalls and runs, plus paddocks and pastures. Maintains a competitive show team competing at schooling and AA-rated shows across Texas.

trainers.name: Marley
trainers.bio: Instructor and contact at Hunters Chase Farms, leading a competitive show team across Texas.

name: Hill Country Equestrian Lodge
description: Located at a historic ranch headquarters adjoining a 5,500-acre natural area in Bandera, Texas — the Cowboy Capital of the World. The facility spans 6,000 acres with miles of trails through rugged Texas Hill Country terrain. Offers western and English riding, trail riding, cattle sorting clinics, summer riding camps, and horsemanship instruction for all skill levels. Private entrance to Hill Country State Natural Area.

name: Bit and Bridle Stables
description: A ranch in the beautiful Texas Hill Country near Harper, TX offering privately scheduled trail rides, private lessons, and equine therapy. Family-friendly with experienced, patient instructors. Instruction available from beginner through advanced levels including bareback riding. Beautiful scenic views on ranch property.

trainers.name: Tim
trainers.bio: Owner and instructor known for welcoming demeanor and experience with riders of all levels.

trainers.name: Vanessa
trainers.bio: Owner and instructor praised for patience and teaching ability, specializing in beginner through advanced lessons.

name: Red Horse Ranch
description: A family-operated boarding facility in Dripping Springs where horses have large pens with plenty of room to run and play, lots of trees for shade, and large run-in barns. Owners Dave and Lisa Bierbower personally care for all horses. Known locally as the no-rib ranch due to the excellent condition of boarders' horses. Full board starting at $495/month with temporary boarding also available.

name: Trails of Horseshoe Bay Equestrian Center
description: A world class equestrian center in the heart of Texas Hill Country at Horseshoe Bay on Lake LBJ. Features a professional exercise arena, round pen, stables, and access to an extensive trail system. Set within a premier equestrian community where residents can keep horses on their acreage lots along the beautiful Hill Country preserve.

name: Lobos Horse Ranch & Stables
description: Lobos Horse Ranch & Stables provides full-care, self-care, and partial-care horse boarding located near Fort Worth, Dallas, Arlington, and Burleson in the Dallas-Fort Worth area. Full-care boarding includes turnout, hay and feed, water service, stall cleaning, free trailer parking, horse wash station, and arena and round pen access. The facility specializes in horse training, including roping horses and colt starting, and also offers horses for sale.

name: White Rock Stables
description: White Rock Stables offers exclusive full care horse boarding nestled in the beautiful Lake Highlands foothills surrounded by more than 350 acres of pastoral trails. The beautiful 14-acre ranch near Flag Pole Hill and within a mile north of White Rock Lake has been in operation for over 90 years. Private boarding is available by appointment only, offering stalls or stalls with paddocks and daily turnout.

trainers.name: Julie Rentfro Wilson
trainers.bio: Julie Rentfro Wilson started riding at White Rock Stables before she could walk. Her mother boarded horses at White Rock, so the love of horses was inherited at an early age.

name: Three Oaks Equestrian Center
description: Three Oaks Equestrian Center is a quaint location in the middle of town near DFW Airport with access to extensive trail systems. The facility features a 14-stall barn with a lighted sand arena that is regularly groomed, two large pastures with a pond, and two tack rooms. Full-care boarding services include all-day and night turnout, individual stalls, personalized feeding, fresh coastal hay, and clean water. Private lessons are available for lesson horse riders and horse owners, along with horse training and tune-ups.

trainers.name: Megan
trainers.bio: Provides lessons and training at Three Oaks Equestrian Center, offering instruction for lesson horse riders and horse owners.

name: Southlake Equestrian
description: Southlake Equestrian is a premiere equestrian facility in Southlake, TX offering riding lessons, competition equestrian training, and horse boarding serving the Dallas, Fort Worth, and DFW region. The facility provides safe, healthy, and experienced school-master type horses that teach each student how to become a seasoned equestrian through their lesson academy.

name: Summer Hill Farms
description: Summer Hill Farms is a full-service hunter/jumper riding program located in Flower Mound, TX, dedicated to providing quality equestrian experiences. The facility is staffed by professional coaches committed to developing young horses and new riders into competitive athletes. The program has produced multiple National USEF Horse of the Year winners, Zone Horse of the Year winners, Pony Finals Winners, and scholarship-winning riders. Services include training, showing, sales, lessons, and IEA (Interscholastic Equestrian Association) teams.

name: Monarch Stables Austin
description: Monarch Stables offers a fresh approach to hunter jumper training and instruction for all levels of riders, and full service boarding for our equine partners. Located on 56 acres, 13 miles from downtown Austin along tree-lined Onion Creek.

trainers.name: Kalli Smith
trainers.bio: Specializes in correct flat work in developing finished show horses and skillful child and adult riders.

trainers.name: Amy Gangstad
trainers.bio: Focused on hunter jumper training for all levels of riders.

name: Coraggio Equestrian
description: Hunter/Jumper barn in Austin, Texas Hill Country, specializing in a personalized training program for you and your horse in a private, relaxed atmosphere. Features a lighted 250x150 outdoor arena, 75x75 covered arena, and 100+ acres of shaded trails with ride-out access to Jester King Brewery.

name: Paul Cates Stable
description: Family owned and operated stable of American Saddlebred Horses offering training, riding lessons, boarding, sales, and stud & foal services. The greater Austin area's premier Saddleseat training and lesson stable, serving Cedar Park, Leander, Georgetown, and Hutto.

trainers.name: Paul Cates
trainers.bio: Professional trainer since 1987, specializing in American Saddlebred horses and riders for competition.

name: Guarded Heart Farm
description: Guarded Heart Farm Horse Boarding & Equestrian Center is a 35-acre property convenient to Austin, Georgetown, Cedar Park, Hutto and Round Rock—approximately 10 minutes from the Williamson County Expo Center. Features a spring-fed pond, on-site barn manager, and 24/7 monitoring. Boarding options include stall, run, and pasture board.

name: Highland Park Riding School
description: An all-women's equestrian sanctuary focused on English Hunter/Jumper riding. A space where women can grow in strength, confidence, and connection—both with each other and with horses. Features compassionate, highly trained instructors in a safe, supportive environment for women 30+.

name: Esoteric Farm
description: A Boutique Equestrian Training Center offering full care Hunter Jumper boarding in NE Austin. Serving Austin, Pflugerville, Westlake, Round Rock, Cedar Park, Manor, Leander, and Lakeway. USEF, USHJA, and THJA affiliated.

trainers.name: Kersten Levine
trainers.bio: Head Trainer. Started riding at age two in Hawaii. Qualified for pony finals by age 11 and has competed at state, national, and international Grand Prix levels. Founded Esoteric Farm in 2013. Her Grand Prix horse Entrepreneur won the World Cup series for the Pacific League.

trainers.name: Nancy Ansley
trainers.bio: Trainer and Barn Manager. Multiple state championships through the Arizona Horse Exhibitors Association. Competed at the National Medal Finals at Harrisburg and the Washington International Horse Show. Holds a Doctorate in Veterinary Medicine from Auburn University.

name: Silver Oak Stable
description: A 26-acre private boarding facility located on the North Shore of Long Island, New York, offering hunters/jumpers training in a comfortable, relaxed, high-quality environment. Features a 110'x225' indoor arena with TravelRight dust-free footing, two outdoor rings, 24 stalls, a 72-foot six-horse hot walker with interior round pen, and 21 grass paddocks.

name: Gold Coast Equestrian
description: A premier 76-stall equestrian facility in Old Brookville, Long Island, equipped with one of the largest indoor arenas in Nassau County, two outdoor arenas, and top-notch lesson and training programs. Offers boarding, riding lessons, sales, leasing, horse showing, and IEA competition.

name: Bethpage Equestrian Center
description: A full-service equestrian facility adjacent to the beautiful Bethpage State Park on Long Island, offering horse boarding and training in both Western and English styles, along with a wide array of programs for children and adults including riding lessons, trail rides, barrel practices, day camps, and pony parties.

name: New York Equestrian Center
description: A facility with nearly 100 years of history originally built in the 1920s, located adjacent to the 775-acre Hempstead Lake State Park in West Hempstead. Offers horseback riding lessons, trail rides, summer camp, after-school programs, pony parties, and equine studies curriculum. A beloved Long Island equestrian institution since the 1920s.

name: North Shore Equestrian Center
description: The premier equestrian center on the north shore of Long Island for learning how to ride horses, featuring beautiful modern stalls, breathtaking grounds, and top instructors. Home to the LIU NCAA Equestrian Team and partners with multiple Long Island summer camps and LIU Veterinary School.

name: Choya Farms
description: Designed to be a sanctuary for horses and people alike in Manorville, New York, Choya Farms offers full board options, barns for rent, an indoor arena, riding lessons for all levels, and horse training. The facility also hosts the Great Strides Long Island therapeutic horseback riding program and emphasizes a family-oriented atmosphere.

name: Skye Acres Equestrian Center
description: Equestrian training and sales facility on 12 perfect acres nestled in eastern Long Island in East Patchogue. Offers horse boarding, training, lessons, leasing, and sales. Open seven days a week from 7am to 10pm to serve boarders and students across all skill levels.

name: Gray Horse Farm Hamptons
description: Founded in 2001, GHF Hamptons is a full-service show barn on a 115-acre premier facility in Bridgehampton featuring two indoor rings, three outdoor sand rings, a Grand Prix field, a polo field, and top-of-the-line footing. Offers year-round and seasonal private lessons, boarding, horse leases, and a Pony Camp running June through August.

name: Stony Hill Stables
description: A USHJA-recognized, state-of-the-art equestrian facility established over 60 years ago in Amagansett, just a mile from the ocean. Operated by the Hotchkiss family and specializing in dressage, hunter, and jumper disciplines. Head trainer Wick Hotchkiss is a Grand Prix rider and USDF bronze, silver, and gold medalist. Offers complete board with training, lessons, summer camp, and horsemanship clinics.

name: Longacre MB
description: Nestled in the picturesque landscape of Bridgehampton South in Water Mill, Longacre MB Equestrian Center offers an unparalleled experience for horse enthusiasts of all ages. Features a 15,000 sq ft indoor arena, two outdoor all-weather arenas, a grass ring, 27 oversized 14x14 stalls, 14 turnout paddocks, and trail riding. Headed by USHJA Certified Trainer Raquel Batto with over 20 years of experience.

name: Razor Creek Stables
description: A full board facility located in the J-town area of Louisville, offering a warm and friendly atmosphere to riders and boarders of all ages and experience levels. Horses are turned out 6 days a week weather permitting, with twice-daily hay and grain feeding using homegrown hay and 12% sweet feed.

name: Spring Run Farm
description: A small, family-oriented barn in Prospect, KY specializing in dressage, eventing, show jumping, cross country, basic hunt seat, equitation, and jumping. Home to approximately 70 horses across five barns with hundreds of acres of trails and fields. Owner Susan Perellis is the only instructor in Kentucky certified by both the United States Eventing Association and the US Dressage Federation.

trainers.name: Susan L. Perellis
trainers.bio: Teaching since the early 1970s, A-level Pony Club graduate and national examiner. Competed through Grand Prix dressage and 3-day events including Rolex and Radnor. Level IV Pony Club Examiner, Level III USEF Eventing Instructor, and USEF Dressage Instructor through 4th Level.

trainers.name: Sam McLean
trainers.bio: Competed in hunter/jumpers through high school in upstate New York. Joined Spring Run Farm in 2017. Currently competing at training level eventing.

name: Louisville Equestrian Center
description: Louisville's premier full-service equestrian facility with 78 matted stalls, offering riding lessons, boarding, horse sales, summer camps, pony parties, and horse show services. Features two climate-controlled indoor arenas, six indoor grooming areas, an automatic fly spray system, and 24-hour security. Multiple boarding options from pasture to full board with training.

trainers.name: Don Bridges
trainers.bio: Director of Training at Louisville Equestrian Center, overseeing customized daily care and training programs for boarding and training clients.

name: Bannon Woods Farm
description: A private hunter/jumper facility in southern Louisville owned and operated by the Denzik family. The beautiful 41-acre farm is surrounded by 5,000 acres of Jefferson County Forest, offering miles of trails, spacious indoor and outdoor arenas for year-round riding and training. Services include full care boarding, riding lessons, summer camp, mare care, and lay-up service with an equine veterinarian on staff.

name: Rock Creek Riding Club
description: Louisville's historic riding club established in 1929, dedicated to teaching horsemanship and promoting educational opportunities involving horses, horseback riding, and horse showing. Located across from Seneca Park on 7 acres of the original 37-acre property with a Victorian clubhouse. Famous for their annual Saddlebred horse show and access to Olmsted-designed bridle paths in Seneca and Cherokee Parks.

name: Temple Equestrian Center
description: A full-service horse boarding and care facility in Oldham County, Kentucky offering boarding, training, riding lessons, horse sales, and trail riding on 2,500 acres of trails and farmland. Horses receive customized training and five-star treatment with daily care and 24-hour video surveillance. All skill levels welcome for lessons.

name: Circle G Stables
description: Tucked away in a private yet convenient centrally located picturesque setting in Shelby County, Kentucky. A beautiful, serene, comfortable, and enjoyable environment for you and your horse friends, where horses and riders are treated like family. Offering horse boarding, horse lay-up, and pasture boarding with run-in sheds.

name: Miron Equine Center
description: Located in the heart of Simpsonville, Kentucky, Miron Equine Center provides world-class care for horses with a clients-first, integrity-driven approach. Specializing in broodmare care, foaling services, rehabilitation, show and sport horse boarding, and retirement services. Both indoor full care and outdoor full care boarding available with custom rehabilitation plans.

name: The Farm at Grace Hill
description: A Kentucky farm and horse boarding facility in Crestwood offering large stalls, paddocks, and a round pen with on-site owner supervision. Located approximately 1 mile from I-71 near Louisville. The farm also offers life coaching and equine-assisted coaching experiences. No stallions accepted.

trainers.name: Jessica Wuchterl
trainers.bio: Owner of The Farm at Grace Hill with over two decades of business experience. Equine-assisted life coach who uses horses as mirrors for human behavior and transformation. Daughter of an equine veterinarian.

name: Circle Bar C Ranch
description: For over 20 years, Circle Bar C Ranch has been building a top-tier training and boarding facility in La Grange, Kentucky. The ranch features over 100 acres of lush pasture, 12x12 stalls with individual paddocks, three hot and cold wash racks, Nelson automatic feeders and waterers, and therapeutic stall flooring. The renowned Benton Jackson Arena spans over 55,000 sq. ft. with premium Kiser footing and is recognized as one of the best indoor arenas in Kentucky. Offers full-service boarding, pasture boarding, lessons, trail rides, and overnight trips.

name: River Chase Farm
description: River Chase Farm is a full-service equestrian facility in the heart of picturesque Northern Virginia horse country. The farm offers full-service horse boarding, training, and instruction from a team of passionate trainers. The property features an indoor ring with a heated viewing area, beautiful ride-out paths and trails, and spacious turnout fields. Whether you are a serious competitor or a weekend hobbyist, River Chase Farm welcomes all riders into their horse-crazy family.

name: Orchard Manor Show Stables
description: Orchard Manor Show Stables is a premier Hunter/Jumper/Equitation boarding, training, and showing facility situated on a beautiful historic 35-acre equestrian estate in the heart of Leesburg, Virginia. The property features show-quality indoor and outdoor rings, acres of lush paddocks, and access to beautifully maintained equestrian trails. Expert head trainer Jason Yannitello leads a dedicated program tailored for boarding riders of all levels, with a focus on mastering true horsemanship in and out of the ring.

name: Cavallo Farm
description: Cavallo Farm is a boarding facility specializing in Hunter/Jumper training, located just five minutes from the Brambleton Town Center and minutes from the Dulles Toll Road and Route 50. Owned by Michael and Marie Bertozzi, the farm offers a show-oriented lesson program and accepts new clients through the Cavallo Riding Center. The farm does not offer trail rides or one-time lessons, focusing instead on dedicated equestrian development for serious riders.

name: Clairvaux
description: Clairvaux is a full-service Hunter/Jumper riding academy and show stable in Northern Virginia where equestrian dreams take flight. The facility fosters a supportive community where riders of all skill levels develop lasting connections through their shared passion for horses. Clairvaux offers expert training programs for horse owners, horseback riding lessons with access to experienced lesson horses, and show preparation. The stable does not offer trail rides or horses for hire.

name: Morven Park International Equestrian Center
description: Morven Park International Equestrian Center is a premier competition venue serving all levels of competition from beginners to some of the world's top riders. The center hosts multiple disciplines year-round, including hunter/jumper shows, dressage competitions, carriage driving events, and the Morven Park Spring Horse Trials (eventing). Evolved from the renowned Morven Park International Equestrian Institute that operated from 1963 to 1991, the facility continues its legacy as one of the top equestrian venues in Northern Virginia. Please note the center does not board horses or offer riding lessons.

name: Picturesque Farm
description: Picturesque Farm is a 36-acre equestrian facility situated on the south side of Warrenton, Virginia. The 28-stall show barn features a heated and air-conditioned lounge and bathroom, two outdoor all-weather sand arenas, a 140x75-foot indoor arena, and 110 acres of trails at an adjacent 200-acre annex. With over 20 years of experience, the farm offers horse sales and leasing (averaging 100+ horses and ponies per year), year-round riding lessons, VHSA/BHSA recognized show programs, summer camps for ages 6-15, and show team opportunities. Voted Best Riding Lesson Facility 2019 and Best Equestrian Facility 2022 by Warrenton Lifestyle.

name: MSF Equestrians
description: MSF Equestrians LLC is based out of Black Horse Farm and Stables at the historic Ashland Farm in Warrenton, Virginia. The property features an indoor arena, outdoor arena, and miles of beautiful trails in the Warrenton Hunt territory. The facility offers Hunter, Jumper, and Equitation lessons and training rides for all ages and skill levels. Ship-ins are welcome. The program is run by Mary Ficklin and emphasizes quality horsemanship across all experience levels.

name: Foxrock Stables
description: Foxrock Stables offers instruction by award-winning trainers and traditional equine accommodations conveniently located in Middleburg, Virginia. With over 30 years of experience in teaching, training, and competing, the barn provides custom-tailored English riding lessons for novice and experienced riders alike. The facility specializes in full-care stall boarding with daily turnout, off-the-track Thoroughbred rehabilitation and training, foxhunting programs (seasonal), and trainer support for A-rated and local horse shows. The barn participates in the National Capital Equitation League.

name: Wellington Equestrian Center Inc
description: State of the art equestrian center in Wellington, FL with complete infrastructure and high technology for horses, riders, trainers, and grooms. Built in 2017, the facility features 60 modern 12x12 stalls, three arena types including insulated covered, sand outdoor, and turf outdoor arenas, an oval walker, Theraplate, Aquatrainer, hacking field, and wash facilities. Two accommodation houses (1,144 sq ft each, sleeping up to 16 people) are also available. Located steps from the Winter Equestrian Festival and Global Dressage Festival.

name: Casperey Stables
description: Operating since 1982, Casperey Stables emphasizes quality horse boarding and excellent care with customizable boarding options designed around the safety, care, and comfort of the horses. Located just 10 minutes from WEF, the facility offers 12x12 stalls with rubber mats or wooden floors, 2 lighted irrigated rings, round pen, turnouts, hot/cold wash racks, trails on property, ceiling fans, auto waterers, and auto fly spray. Private tack rooms available.

name: IDA Farm
description: Located in Wellington, Florida — the Equestrian Capital of the World — IDA Farm provides professional equestrian training and boarding in a relaxed yet professional atmosphere. The 22.5-acre facility welcomes riders of all levels in jumping and dressage disciplines. It features 130 horse stalls across 6 barns, 36 paddocks, an all-weather outdoor regulation dressage arena with mirrors, a 100x200 all-weather jumping arena, two covered arenas, a 130x250 multi-purpose outdoor arena, 3 round pens, an exercise track with training hill, equine massage therapy services, and laundry facilities.

trainers.name: Lauren Knopp
trainers.bio: FEI Dressage Trainer specializing in Young Riders programs.

name: Diamante Farms Dressage
description: Nestled in the heart of Wellington, Florida's equestrian capital, Diamante Farms is a newly designed, state-of-the-art dressage facility spanning 20 acres. Founded by owner Terri Kane, the facility emphasizes equine excellence and serves competitive Grand Prix riders and amateur enthusiasts alike. World-class arenas with professional footing set the stage for top-level dressage training.

trainers.name: Terri Kane
trainers.bio: Owner and visionary leader of Diamante Farms with a focus on dressage excellence.

trainers.name: Devon Kane
trainers.bio: Dressage trainer at Diamante Farms.

trainers.name: Kevin Kohmann
trainers.bio: International dressage competitor and trainer at Diamante Farms.

name: Top Gallant Farms
description: A private, peaceful, hurricane-safe barn just 15 minutes from WEF offering dry, partial, and full care boarding for the 2025-26 season or annual. The facility features 7 fully matted 12x14 stalls with 2 fans each, a 280x85 arena with Performance Footing, dressage letters and jumps, 3 permanent paddocks, 3 wash racks with RO water, a viewing station, RV hook-up (60 Amp), Wi-Fi, and security cameras.

name: Poinciana Farm
description: A private dressage training center located on C Road in Loxahatchee Groves, this 13-acre horse park sets a new standard in dressage horse boarding. Horses' health and happiness, facility care, and maintaining a pleasant environment for equine and human clients are the priorities. The facility features 60 stalls (12x12), a regulation-sized covered arena, a double-wide outdoor arena with all-weather footing, a walking trail around a lake, a covered 8-horse walker with fans, open and covered round pens, grass paddocks, a climate-controlled vet room, and farrier workspace. All client trainers welcome. Dogs allowed with a designated dog park.

name: Invictus Farms FL
description: Invictus Farm is a private equestrian facility located in a gated equestrian community just 15 minutes from the Winter Equestrian Festival (WEF), Global Dressage Facility, and International Polo Club. The 5-acre farm offers boarding, training, and lessons in dressage and hunter/jumper disciplines. Facilities include 2 barns with 10 stalls, a regulation-size irrigated dressage arena with mirrors and full set of jumps, a full-size round pen, private canal trails for hacking, air-conditioned tack rooms and feed rooms, fly control, and trailer parking. Beginners to advanced riders welcome; children ages 4 and up accepted.

name: Palm Beach Equine Sports Complex
description: Wellington's Premier Sport Horse Destination on 47 acres with large stalls and 6 arenas, located strides from WEF and AGDF. The facility is committed to providing the perfect conditions and atmosphere that help riders reach their equestrian goals. Features include 3 hunter/jumper rings (one double-sized), 3 dressage rings (one covered with mirrors), a 3/4-mile private exercise track, a 17-acre lake, groomed bridle paths, dedicated turn-out paddocks, on-site laundry facilities, a private lounge/club atmosphere, on-site staff apartments, and a manure composting system.

name: Raven Ridge Farm
description: Raven Ridge Farm is an equestrian facility dedicated to providing a safe, high-end space for horse boarding, care, and training. The farm specializes in quality show hunters, jumpers, and equitation horses, preparing riders to successfully compete in national horse shows from Short Stirrup to Grand Prix. The facility offers full-service boarding and training programs with two shared rings, shared round pen, and paddock use. Located in the Wellington/Loxahatchee Groves area.

name: GNC Farm
description: GNC Farm was designed with both horses and riders in mind, featuring a barn with 30-foot ceilings for optimal airflow and natural light and spacious 16-foot-wide aisles. A Nordic-inspired luxury design with Saratoga European stall fronts. Provides safe, private, luxurious horse boarding, show coaching, show jumping classes, and horse sales in Wellington, Florida. The facility serves dressage and show jumping athletes with boarding, training, coaching, and horse development programs.

name: HaddenLoch
description: HaddenLoch is a premier hunter/jumper boarding and training facility established in 1994, located minutes from Ponte Vedra Beach, Nocatee, and St. Augustine. The property features an all-weather sand arena with a full course of polymer jumps, automatic fly systems, and meticulous daily care including five-times-daily hay feeding and monthly psyllium supplementation.

name: Diamond D Boarding Stables
description: Diamond D Boarding Stables is a well-established horse boarding facility in Jacksonville, Florida, offering quality care and boarding services for horses in a peaceful country setting. The stable provides a welcoming environment for horses and their owners in the Northeast Florida region.

name: Hidden Fields Private Stable
description: Hidden Fields Private Stable is a family-run equestrian facility in Jacksonville, Florida, owned by Debbie Field. Offering a unique blend of horse care, riding lessons, and equine experiences, the stable is open by appointment and specializes in creating memorable experiences for all ages including birthday parties, field trips, and seasonal activities.

name: Jacksonville Equestrian Center
description: Jacksonville Equestrian Center is Florida's premier equestrian venue, hosting USEF and USHJA-sanctioned horse shows, tournaments, and community events year-round. The facility features several indoor and outdoor arenas and is conveniently located near I-10, I-95, and I-295, serving both seasoned equestrians and newcomers to the sport.

name: Coriander Farm
description: Coriander Farm is Jacksonville's leading riding school, offering hunter/jumper instruction for all levels from beginner through national show competition. Located on Joda Lane South, the farm provides school horse lessons with safe and gentle horses and ponies, full board and training with six-day-a-week turnout and grooming, and show preparation from local one-day shows to the national level.

name: Windemere Equestrian Center
description: Windemere Equestrian Center is an 18-acre full-service show, lesson, breeding, and boarding facility in Jacksonville established in 1994. The center features a 24-stall indoor barn plus a 12-horse barn, two large lighted outdoor arenas, a full jump course, covered wash barn, and spacious turnout, offering hunter/jumper and Appaloosa breed programs at all competitive levels.

name: La Rose Performance Horses
description: La Rose Performance Horses is a comprehensive equestrian facility established in 2012, situated on 3105 CR 121 in Jacksonville, forty minutes from the beaches and fifteen minutes from the Jacksonville Equestrian Center. The facility welcomes riders of all disciplines and levels, featuring a covered 100x150 arena extending to 100x230, outdoor arena with full jump course, round pen, hot walker, Theraplate therapy equipment, and wash racks with hot and cold water.

name: BV Equestrian Show Stables
description: BV Equestrian Show Stables is a premier hunter/jumper training and show facility located on 20 acres in St. Augustine. The 26-stall facility offers USEF and USHJA-sanctioned competition preparation with structured training programs, requiring boarders to participate in a minimum of eight horse shows annually at venues including Ocala, Aiken, Venice, Tryon, and Blowing Rock.

name: M & M Hunter Jumpers
description: M & M Hunter Jumpers is a boutique-style hunter/jumper training stable in St. Augustine run by Michelle Moen, who brings over 30 years of hunter/jumper experience. The 133-acre property features a brand-new 250x180 fully lighted indoor clay/sand jump arena, a 200x100 outdoor sand arena, a cross-country log course, miles of trails, 18 large paddocks with daily individual turnout, and three indoor wash racks with hot and cold water.

name: Southern Pines Equestrian Center
description: Southern Pines Equestrian Center is a 30-acre equestrian facility in St. Augustine offering English and Western riding instruction for all ages and experience levels. The property features a large outdoor arena with premium footing, a grass arena with jumps, two round pens, covered and uncovered wash areas, property trails, spacious 12x12 stalls, and a climate-controlled classroom. The center also offers HomeSchool Horse Club programs, birthday celebrations, seasonal camps, vacation rental accommodations, and photography sessions.

name: Bella Cavalli Farms & Vineyard
description: A 50-acre French Country-style equestrian estate in the Santa Ynez Valley, just west of Solvang. Bella Cavalli offers full boarding, pasturing, and a renowned lay-up, rehabilitation, and reconditioning center for horses recovering from demanding activities. The training facility specializes in reining, dressage, jumping, and English disciplines, with a half-mile conditioning track, irrigated pastures, and large comfortable stalls alongside an on-site vineyard.

name: Happy N Ranch
description: Located in the Santa Ynez Valley between Buellton and Solvang, Happy N Ranch is a retirement, rehabilitation, and pasture care facility established over 33 years ago. Owner Lisa Novatt is a Licensed and Registered Veterinary Technician, bringing professional equine health expertise to every horse in her care. The ranch features scenic grounds with heritage oak trees and specializes in senior horses and those needing a peaceful recovery environment.

name: California Horse Center
description: Situated at the historic Flag Is Up Farms in Solvang, California Horse Center is a premier boarding and training facility rooted in Monty Roberts' natural horsemanship philosophy. The state-of-the-art 90' x 275' covered arena, EuroXciser, mountain trail course, bridle paths, and expansive pastures create an enriching environment for horses. Customized feeding programs, 24/7 on-site staff, and walkout stalls support the holistic well-being of every horse.

name: Black Mountain Sport Horses
description: Black Mountain Sport Horses is a dedicated hunter-jumper training facility in Santa Barbara specializing in developing riders and horses from first lessons to national-level Grand Prix competition. The program emphasizes world-class horsemanship and produces finished horses for the highest levels of the sport. With experienced head instructors and a structured competition-focused curriculum, it serves ambitious junior and adult competitors throughout the Santa Barbara region.

name: Westerly Stud Farm & Equestrian Center
description: A historic 250-acre property in the heart of the Santa Ynez Valley offering premier boarding, training, rehabilitation, lay-up, young horse programs, and retirement services. Westerly Stud boasts a 60-foot covered round pen, large arena with premium positrac footing, EuroXciser, hot and cold wash rack, dry and irrigated turnouts, lush green pastures, and miles of manicured trails. Located just 10 minutes from a major equine veterinary hospital, it provides exceptional care in a quiet, private setting.

name: Santa Barbara Stables
description: Nestled on Hilltop Ranch, a breathtaking 350-acre avocado estate in Carpinteria with panoramic Pacific Ocean views, Santa Barbara Stables is a premier West Coast training and boarding facility. Trainer Rebecca Atwater offers customized programs for hunters, jumpers, and dressage riders in three dedicated rings with quality footing. Horses enjoy daily grass turnouts, wash racks with hot water, a European walker, polo field access, and miles of private trails—a rare find in Southern California.

name: Friend's Stable & Orchard
description: A small, private horse boarding facility tucked within one of Ojai's historic citrus orchards, Friend's Stable & Orchard offers a peaceful and secluded environment for show horses, trail horses, arena companions, and retired equines alike. The facility provides top-notch personalized care with boarding options tailored to a variety of needs, set against the scenic backdrop of Ojai's orchards and hills.

name: Canty Rune Training Stables
description: Located in Oak View alongside San Antonio Creek with stunning mountain views, Canty Rune Training Stables is run by the dynamic duo of Laurie May Canty and Sarah Rune. Just 15 minutes from Ventura and 10 minutes from Ojai, this boarding and training facility offers instruction in eventing, jumping, dressage, and all-around horsemanship for adults and youth. Laurie holds a British Horse Society Instructor Certificate from 1970 and has nearly five decades of experience, while Sarah brings a passion for helping riders of all levels achieve their goals.

name: The Stable On The Woods
description: Aiken's elite boarding and training facility set on 70 acres with a 26-stall courtyard-style barn. Offers customized programs in dressage, hunter/jumper, eventing, and foxhunting, with direct access to the famous 2,000-acre Hitchcock Woods trail system. Features a full-size dressage arena with mirrors and a dedicated show jumping arena.

name: The Stables at New Bridge Polo
description: A full-service equestrian boarding facility within the New Bridge Polo & Country Club. Features a 24-stall center aisle barn with 12x12 matted stalls, fans, and a fly spray system. Amenities include a 30,000 sq ft all-weather jumping arena, regulation dressage arena with GGT footing, miles of bridle paths, and a comfortable rider's lounge with laundry.

name: Magnolia Meadow Stables
description: A full-service equestrian facility on 30 private acres within the Bridle Creek Equestrian Community in Aiken, SC. The 17-stall barn caters to eventers, hunters, jumpers, and dressage riders with a full-size riding arena, irrigated grass jump field, cross-country course with water jump, jog track, round pen, and miles of private trails.

name: Jumping Branch Farm
description: A premier eventing training facility on 100+ acres in Aiken, SC, surrounded by trees, trails, and two ponds with large turnout fields. Best known for its Starter through Preliminary cross-country courses that are inviting yet challenging. Hosts schooling shows including dressage, combined tests, horse trials, and derbies.

name: DFG Stables
description: A professional hunter/jumper training and boarding facility operated by USEF National Equestrian of the Year Daniel Geitner, located in the heart of Aiken, SC. DFG Stables competes at the highest levels of the hunter/jumper circuit with a strong record at prestigious shows nationwide, with a seasonal base in Ocala, FL.

name: Ligara Farm
description: A beautifully maintained boarding facility on 24 acres in the horse-rich 302 corridor of Aiken, SC. Offers full and self-care stall options as well as pasture board. Features 8 fully matted stalls, a hot water wash rack, jumps, perimeter riding track, and trails — just 8 miles from Hitchcock Woods.

name: Vaughn Equestrian
description: A holistic equestrian facility in Aiken, SC focused on the well-being of horse and rider. With over 55 years of combined staff experience, Vaughn Equestrian offers boarding, dressage training, jump training, and retraining projects, as well as equine Reiki massage healing. A welcoming environment known for client camaraderie and individualized care.

name: Red Horse Stables
description: An eventing and dressage barn located within the Wire Road Polo Association in Aiken, SC. The main barn features six 12x12 matted stalls with a tack room, hot water wash stall, and washer/dryer, plus a lower barn with two additional stalls. Full board starts at $775/month. Turnout across five paddocks is tailored to each horse's needs.

name: Kelsey Farm
description: A beloved family barn in the backcountry of Greenwich, established in 1949. Kelsey Farm offers horseback riding lessons, full boarding, a shared pony program, summer camp, and showing for riders of all ages and levels. The property features year-round paddock turnout with run-in sheds, both indoor and outdoor arenas, and a miniature cross-country course. With eleven box stalls and more than 75 years of tradition, Kelsey Farm fosters a strong foundation in horsemanship in a warm, family-oriented atmosphere.

name: Mead Farm
description: One of Connecticut's oldest equestrian establishments, Mead Farm has been welcoming riders to its Stamford property since 1928. The farm offers a comprehensive and challenging riding experience within a casual atmosphere, providing year-round lessons for children and adults, full boarding, horse leasing and sales, birthday parties, and summer camps. Instruction covers all levels, with pricing of $135 per private half-hour lesson. The farm maintains both indoor and outdoor riding areas along with party room and grooming facilities.

name: Silvermine Farm
description: A full-service Hunter/Jumper show stable nestled in the heart of Norwalk's historic Silvermine neighborhood. Silvermine Farm offers lessons, full-care boarding, leases, sales, and a competitive IEA Team for school-age riders. The facility supports a network of veterinary, farrier, and specialist services including equine massage therapy to ensure peak horse performance. Open Tuesday through Sunday, the farm cultivates riders at all levels from beginners through competitive show riders.

name: Fairfield County Hunt Club
description: A nationally recognized private equestrian country club in Westport, Connecticut, offering a world-class hunter/jumper program for riders from beginners to high-level competitors. The 34-acre facility features 120 box stalls across 6 barns, two heated indoor rings (100x200 and 90x250), three outdoor rings with state-of-the-art footing, a four-place hot walker, 9 all-weather paddocks, 7 wash stalls, and a regulation polo field. A professional training team including Head of Training Aline Domaingo and hunter trainer Jenny Martin-Rudaz delivers customized programs year-round.

name: Serenity Show Stable
description: A full-service show stable located on the historic 53-acre Marchant Farm property in Redding, Connecticut. Serenity Show Stable offers training and lessons for riders of all levels, from beginners through competitors at prestigious AA-rated horse shows nationwide. The facility features an all-weather indoor riding ring, outdoor riding ring, a dedicated jumping field, multiple paddocks, extensive trail access, grooming areas, tack rooms, and large open-air stalls. The stable emphasizes both technical riding skill development and competitive preparation.

name: Sunset Hill Farm Equestrian Center
description: A privately owned equestrian boarding stable located in the picturesque countryside of Redding, Connecticut. Sunset Hill Farm Equestrian Center provides boarding and riding services in a serene rural setting, offering personalized horse care and a welcoming community for horse owners and riders. The facility is situated on Sunset Hill Road in the heart of Redding's equestrian corridor, making it convenient to the network of local trails and riding paths throughout the region.

name: Whimsy Brook Farm
description: A holistic equestrian facility in Redding, Connecticut, operating since 1972 under the philosophy of "Where Science and Horsemanship Join Up." Whimsy Brook Farm offers full-service horse boarding, riding instruction in hunt seat, dressage, and western disciplines, horse training, and U.S. Pony Club programming. The farm also provides comprehensive equine wellness services including massage, acupuncture, chiropractic, farrier, and dental care. Lesson rates include private one-hour lessons at $199 and semi-private at $165, with package discounts available for boarders.

name: Memory Lane Farm
description: A charming Greenwich equestrian facility situated directly on the Greenwich Riding Trails, offering horseback riding lessons, a kids summer riding program, and cart driving instruction. Memory Lane Farm is ideally positioned for trail riding with access to 150 miles of Greenwich Riding Trails and proximity to the GRTA Horse Show Grounds. The farm also hosts birthday parties and provides dog boarding, making it a true multi-purpose destination. With its trail-side location in Greenwich's backcountry, it offers a uniquely scenic and relaxed riding experience.

name: Los Angeles Equestrian Center
description: The Los Angeles Equestrian Center is a premier 75-acre equestrian facility in Burbank with over 44 years of history. Home to 500 horses across 16 stable areas including the signature Equidome covered arena, the center hosts 30+ annual horse shows, offers boarding with twice-daily feedings, and provides direct access to over 50 miles of Griffith Park riding trails. Nineteen on-site trainers specialize in various disciplines and riding styles.

name: J. Bennett Farms
description: Founded in 1984, J. Bennett Farms is one of the largest stables at the Los Angeles Equestrian Center and the LA home of the American Saddlebred. The farm specializes in Saddle Seat and Western disciplines along with horse driving, and is a premier destination for Saddlebred enthusiasts. Horses are worked six days a week on individualized training schedules. All boarding fees are regulated by the LAEC.

name: The Paddock Riding Club
description: Established in 1987, The Paddock Riding Club is a family-owned premier equestrian center in Los Angeles, adjacent to Griffith Park and its 55 miles of horse trails. The facility features six fully lit riding arenas, a covered Eurociser, Vitafloor therapy plate, rubber-matted aisles, and 11 independent resident trainers covering a wide range of disciplines. Known as 'LA's most thoughtfully maintained equestrian center.'

name: Courtship Ranch
description: In continuous boarding operation since the 1970s, Courtship Ranch is a renowned Shadow Hills facility offering top-quality horse care at affordable prices. Situated backing up to the Angeles National Forest, the ranch provides direct access to over 800 miles of protected riding trails. Specializing in classical and western dressage, natural horsemanship, and working equitation, the ranch features 80+ stalls, three lighted arenas including a fully covered arena, two round pens, and nine turnouts.

name: Farndale Stables
description: Farndale Stables is a full-service training barn located at the Los Angeles Equestrian Center in Burbank, specializing in hunters, jumpers, and equitation. With over 40 years of showing, training, and horse care expertise, trainer Mark Farndale brings international show ring experience to clients of all levels. The barn prepares horses and riders for competition at all levels and welcomes new clients looking to develop in the hunter-jumper discipline.

name: Stoney Point Ranch
description: Stoney Point Ranch is a full-service equestrian center in Chatsworth, nestled near the iconic Stoney Point rock formations and the Santa Susana Mountains. Owner Angel Rodriguez brings nearly 30 years of experience prioritizing horse safety and care. The facility offers Western and English riding lessons for all ages (5 and up), private and semi-private lessons, and boarding with twice-daily feeding and daily stall cleaning. Trails near Rocky Peak off Topanga Canyon are accessible from the property.

name: Mountain Meadows Stables
description: Mountain Meadows Stables is a family-oriented, full-service horse boarding facility set on over 20 acres of landscaped grounds two miles up a scenic canyon road in the west San Fernando Valley. Owned by Helen and Kim Schulte and operating since 1997, the facility offers both English and Western boarding and instruction. The stables feature a resident barn manager employed since 1999, social events throughout the year, and on-call veterinary services through Conejo Valley Equine.

name: Pepper Tree Ranch
description: Serving the San Fernando Valley since 1988, Pepper Tree Ranch is a well-established equestrian facility in Chatsworth offering horse boarding, riding lessons, lease options, and training. The ranch provides box stalls and pipe corrals, all cleaned daily, with lessons available Tuesday through Sunday. A cornerstone of the local equestrian community for nearly four decades, it caters to riders of all levels in both Western and English disciplines.

name: WhiteOak Ranch Chatsworth
description: WhiteOak Ranch is a picturesque horse boarding facility nestled at the base of the Santa Susana Mountains near Stoney Point and the Garden of the Gods in Chatsworth. The property features a 67x200 foot riding arena, a 30-foot round training pen, in-and-out shed row stalls, outdoor pipe corrals with shelters, automatic watering systems, and access to community horse trails through the scenic Chatsworth landscape.

name: Bell Canyon Equestrian Center
description: Nestled in the rolling Simi Hills, Bell Canyon Equestrian Center is one of the few remaining full-service equestrian centers in the San Fernando Valley. The facility offers 80 box stalls and 30 pipe corrals, three arenas with fiber footing groomed and watered daily, a round pen, sunpens and turnouts, plus miles of beautiful chaparral trails for pleasure riding. On-site trainers specialize in hunter, jumper, dressage, and western disciplines, with 24-hour gated security access.

name: Shangri-La Farm Equine
description: Located in Morriston, FL on 115 acres, Shangri-La Farm offers horse boarding and breeding options for all breeds and disciplines. Services include full board, pasture and retirement board, breeding and foaling out, rehab, and stud services.

name: Big Iron Farm
description: Located 15 miles from Ocala in Citra, FL, Big Iron Farm is a 26-acre full-care facility with a laid-back, welcoming atmosphere and live-in staff. The team specializes in caring for performance horses through the upper levels of sport, with experience in FEI grooming and barn management.

name: Eden Farm
description: Established in 1972, Eden Farm is one of the oldest hunter/jumper barns in Ocala/Marion County, Florida. Located just minutes from local schools, the farm offers English riding lessons and training for equestrians participating in competitive show jumping.

name: Lazy J Farm
description: Lazy J Farm is a horse boarding facility in the heart of Northwest Ocala, just minutes from the World Equestrian Center and HITS.
