The facility accepts all varieties of horses except stallions and is backed by 50+ years of combined owner experience. Features 12x12 stalls with automatic waters and fans.

name: Five Phases Farm
description: Five Phases Farm is situated on 60 acres in Ocala, FL at Epona Equine Center, 10 minutes from both the World Equestrian Center and HITS Show Grounds. The facility accommodates hunter/jumpers, eventing horses, CDE horses, racehorses, and dressage horses with top-of-the-line amenities including a horse swimming pool, half-mile track, and covered eurocizer.

trainers.name: Michelle Warro
trainers.bio: Three-day eventer

name: SilverStride Equestrian Center
description: SilverStride Equestrian Center provides a warm and welcoming environment where inseparable bonds between human and horse can blossom into success. The facility offers horse training, boarding, and sales in Ocala, FL, competing in recognized USEA events and USDF competitions around Florida, Region 3, and nationally.

trainers.name: Brie Seltser
trainers.bio: Owner and trainer at SilverStride Equestrian Center, specializing in hunter/jumper and eventing.

name: BREC Dressage
description: BREC Dressage is situated on 35 beautifully manicured acres north of Ocala, FL, and is one of the area's best equestrian training centers. Offering classical dressage training from Training Level through FEI with full-care boarding and 24/7 care.

trainers.name: Tyra Vernon
trainers.bio: FEI-level trainer, USDF Bronze, Silver and Gold medalist, Regional Champion with 15+ years operating BREC Dressage. Trains from Training Level through FEI.

trainers.name: Kerryann Schade
trainers.bio: Co-trainer at BREC Dressage specializing in dressage.

name: Sapphire Equestrian
description: Sapphire Equestrian at Zafiro Farms is a full-service boarding and training facility located on 30 acres in scenic NW Marion County, FL, just minutes from the World Equestrian Center. Offering training from beginner through advanced Grand Prix levels in hunters, jumpers, equitation, and dressage, as well as warmblood sales and thoroughbred consulting.

name: Olive Acres Equestrian Center
description: Olive Acres Equestrian Center is a family-owned, professionally managed equestrian facility offering full-care boarding and professional training focused on horse welfare, consistency, and long-term success. Located near World Equestrian Center and HITS in Ocala. Features a GGT riding arena, irrigated individual paddocks, and multiple well-ventilated barns with Stable Comfort stall mattresses.

trainers.name: Madison Rose
trainers.bio: Head Trainer and Program Manager at Olive Acres Equestrian Center. Background in hunter/jumper training and competition as well as western disciplines. USEF Registered Jumper Judge with experience producing young horses, developing sale horses, and supporting competitive riders. Has qualified multiple horses for Young Horse Championships.

name: Ashcombe Sporthorses
description: Ashcombe Sporthorses, LLC brings over 30 years of experience developing horses and riders for sport in the hunter/jumper ring. Located just 3 miles from World Equestrian Center Ocala, the facility features spacious 12x12 European-front stalls, covered grooming stalls, wash stalls, and multiple arenas. The program operates November through April in Ocala.

name: In The Breeze Ranch
description: In The Breeze Ranch is a full-service equestrian facility located in northwest Tampa on Gardner Road, offering boarding, lessons, trail riding, horse swimming, and special events. Boarders enjoy private stalls with attached paddocks, premium hay, daily stall cleaning, fresh shavings, and access to an illuminated arena. The property features a spring-fed creek and lake trail system, round pen, and two wash racks. From unicorn parties to professional training board, the ranch serves a wide range of equestrian needs in the greater Tampa Bay area.

name: Annwyn Equestrian Center
description: Annwyn Equestrian Center in Seffner, Florida is an elite dressage training facility established in 2009 by Susan Mitchell and Kathleen Petersen. Originally an elite boarding facility, Annwyn has evolved into one of the Tampa Bay area's premier dressage destinations. The center is open daily from 8am to 8pm and prioritizes the well-being of each horse above all else. Led by dressage trainer Kathleen Petersen, the facility offers professional instruction and training for riders pursuing dressage from introductory through upper-level work.

name: Perennial Farm Riding Academy
description: Perennial Farm Riding Academy is a hunter jumper barn situated on the historic Tampa Yacht & Country Club property along Interbay Boulevard, where horses have roamed since the early 1900s. The academy offers riding lessons for all ages and skill levels, summer camps, and horse show opportunities, combining rich tradition with a genuine love for the horse. Open Tuesday through Sunday, Perennial Farm emphasizes safe, solid horsemanship and a fun, welcoming environment for students pursuing the hunter jumper discipline in South Tampa.

name: Hidden Meadow Farms
description: Hidden Meadow Farms is a welcoming hunter jumper facility in Plant City, Florida, offering personalized riding instruction for riders aged 7 and up. Describing itself as a 'non-show show barn,' Hidden Meadow emphasizes a comprehensive approach to horsemanship that goes beyond the saddle — teaching horse care, equipment handling, and teamwork alongside riding fundamentals. The barn fosters a supportive community where riders encourage one another both at home and at local shows. Optional competition opportunities are available at the Sumter Equestrian Center and Pinellas County Hunter Association.

name: Suncoast Stables and Riding Academy
description: Suncoast Stables and Riding Academy, founded in 2007 by owner and trainer Krystle Lee, is a premier multi-discipline equestrian facility in Odessa, Florida. Specializing in American Saddlebreds, Morgans, Arabians, Hackneys, and Roadsters, Suncoast offers instruction in saddleseat, dressage, western, and hunt seat disciplines. The facility features a lighted covered arena, an open arena, stalls, wash racks, and multiple pasture areas. Krystle Lee, a William Woods University Equestrian Science graduate and 2024 UPHA Chapter 16 Horse Person of the Year, leads the training programs. Services include private lessons, horse training, breeding, summer camps, and horse leasing assistance.

name: Whispering Oaks Equestrian Center
description: Whispering Oaks Equestrian Center is a family-friendly facility on Gardner Road in Tampa, Florida, specializing in Morgan Performance Horses and welcoming all breeds. With over 14 years of producing regional, national, and world-level competitors, the center offers instruction in saddle seat, hunt seat equitation, English, western, hunter pleasure, pleasure driving, park saddle, hunter over fences, and barrel racing. The newly renovated barn and riding area serve students from beginner through advanced levels, with a popular Summer Horsemanship Academy program for youth ages 5 to 16.

name: Kingsmeade Farm
description: Kingsmeade Farm is Tampa Bay's premiere hunter jumper equestrian facility, nestled along a quiet country road in Lutz just 20 minutes from downtown Tampa. The property features 35 twelve-by-twelve stalls, a massive 295-by-275-foot lighted indoor arena with a full jump course, six dedicated wash racks with hot and cold water, fly spray systems, ceiling fans, and three air-conditioned boarder tack rooms with electronic keypad locks. Owner and trainer Clair Kellner has produced riders competing at the highest national levels including Junior Hunter Finals, Maclay, Medal Finals, and Pony Finals. Training board, group lessons, and private lessons are all available.

name: Licciardello Stables
description: Licciardello Stables is a family-friendly equestrian facility in Lutz, Florida, specializing in American Saddlebreds, Morgans, and Hackneys with a focus on saddle seat riding and training. The stable provides top-of-the-line care and training for horses and riders, offering show horse training and boarding, competition training, pleasure lessons, and camps. Open six days a week from 9am to 7pm, Licciardello Stables serves riders throughout the Tampa Bay area looking to develop their skills in a warm, supportive environment.

name: Fire River 4 Ranch
description: Fire River 4 Ranch is a spacious horse boarding facility in Plant City, Florida, offering full board services across more than 120 acres of open pasture. The ranch accommodates small to large horse breeds in 10 private stalls, with daily care provided by on-site staff. The expansive acreage allows for exceptional turnout and natural grazing opportunities. Located at 515 E. Keysville Road, Fire River 4 Ranch is ideal for horse owners in the eastern Hillsborough County area seeking a pastoral, attentive boarding environment with room to roam.

name: The Ranch at Cave Creek
description: The Ranch at Cave Creek is a full-service horse boarding facility set on scenic property in Cave Creek, Arizona. Offering large outdoor and covered arenas alongside attentive daily care, the facility provides a clean and welcoming environment for horses and their owners.

name: Sweetwater Stables
description: Sweetwater Stables is a hunter jumper training facility in Cave Creek, Arizona, offering programs for beginners through high-level competitive jumpers. The barn maintains a family atmosphere and competitive show program, with three experienced trainers specializing in hunters, equitation, and jumpers.

name: River Horse Ranch
description: River Horse Ranch, operated by Jennuine Horse LLC, is a Cave Creek boarding and training facility with a horse-first, mindful horsemanship philosophy. The ranch provides anti-inflammatory diet management, 12-hour daily turnout, and 24/7 slow-feeder hay access. Jennifer offers training clinics, groundwork sessions, and lessons for horses and riders of all breeds and disciplines.

name: Desert Palms Equestrian Center
description: Desert Palms Equestrian Center in Cave Creek is a premier show horse training and boarding facility with 36 stalls, two large outdoor arenas, and a round pen. Owner and trainer Michelle Haisch is a USEF 'R' Judge specializing in American Saddlebred show horses. The facility features automatic watering systems, daily stall cleaning, twice-daily feedings, and hosts youth camps and birthday parties.

name: Chaparosa Ranch
description: Chaparosa Ranch is a multi-discipline equestrian facility nestled in Rio Verde, North Scottsdale, just steps from the Tonto National Forest. The property features two riding arenas including one covered arena, individual and trainer barn options, and direct gate access to national forest trails. Just 20 minutes from WestWorld and 40 minutes from Phoenix Sky Harbor, the ranch also offers casita rentals and event hosting.

name: Hawk Ranch Cave Creek
description: Hawk Ranch Cave Creek is a full-service Western equestrian facility in north Phoenix offering 32 stalls, two expansive riding arenas, a round pen, and access to miles of Arizona State Land and Cave Creek Preserve trails. The ranch specializes in cutting, sorting, roping, and performance horse training, and also features RV hookups, trailer parking, and regular events and clinics.

name: Twisted Tree Farm
description: Twisted Tree Farm has been a premier hunter/jumper training and boarding facility in Scottsdale since 1984. Head trainer Janet Hischer brings over 35 years of competitive experience at local, national, and international levels. The farm offers riding lessons from beginner to advanced, summer and holiday camps, an internship program, and sport horses for sale.

name: Serenity Stables
description: Serenity Stables is a full-service horse boarding facility in Chandler, Arizona, independently owned and operated since 2006. The stable offers western, dressage, and English riding lessons with experienced trainers, and features 24/7 staff monitoring, customized feeding schedules, and daily turnout for grazing and socialization. The owner's hands-on approach ensures every horse and rider receives personalized attention.

name: Equine Connections
description: Equine Connections is a four-acre ranch in Gilbert, Arizona, offering horse boarding, riding lessons, equine-assisted learning, and community programs. Owner and equine specialist Virginia McCarthy emphasizes healing and personal growth through horses. The facility welcomes all ages and skill levels and hosts large group events, workshops, and volunteer opportunities.

name: Kerivan Farms
description: Kerivan Farms is a fully equipped English riding facility in Gilbert, Arizona, specializing in hunter/jumper training, dressage, and IEA competition programs. The farm features a large lighted arena with show jumps, a dressage ring, a round pen, five grass turnout pastures, roomy daily-cleaned stalls, and cross ties with misters. Training and lessons available for all ages and skill levels.

name: Suncoast Farms
description: Suncoast Farms is a 40-acre full-care horse boarding facility in San Diego's South Bay, in the Spurling family since 1977. Located at 2805 Hollister Street with direct trail access and proximity to the Pacific Ocean, the ranch offers year-round grass pastures, custom tackrooms, and a calm, quiet environment for horses from youngsters to retirees.

name: D & J Stables
description: D & J Stables is a family-run equestrian facility founded by Dick and Jenny Tynan in 2005, located on the Kimzey Family Ranch in South San Diego. The property offers 25+ modern outdoor stalls with daily care services and sits adjacent to an extensive trail network connecting wilderness, mountain, and coastal beach riding routes.

name: Canyonside Equestrian Center
description: Canyonside Equestrian Center, originally established as Horseman's Park in the early 1970s by Barbara Gerrior, is a non-profit premier riding, training, and boarding facility situated on 15.44 acres within the Los Penasquitos Canyon Preserve. Located just off the 56 Freeway in San Diego, it provides direct access to miles of preserve trails and offers riding lessons and educational programs.

name: Sweetwater Farms
description: Sweetwater Farms is a community-oriented equestrian center in Bonita, California, welcoming riders of all ages and experience levels. Located on Equitation Lane in San Diego County, the facility offers riding academy programs for children and adults, making it a family-friendly destination for both first-time riders and experienced equestrians.

name: Windfall Ranch
description: Windfall Ranch is a drama-free equine boarding, training, and guided trail riding facility tucked in the San Diego mountains near Alpine. Set on the Japatul Valley Road with miles of private accessible trails, the ranch specializes in competitive endurance riding and equitation training under the direction of trainer Natalie Braun.

name: River Valley Equestrian Center
description: River Valley Equestrian Center is a premier 40-acre horse boarding facility in Lakeside, California, offering a serene East County San Diego setting. The property features spacious stalls, lighted arenas, a round pen, polo fields, and extensive trail access, providing a luxury equestrian experience with ample turnout and recreational riding opportunities.

name: Poway Equestrian Center
description: Poway Equestrian Center is a full-service equestrian facility at 14875 Mina de Oro Road in Poway, California, operated by Nicole Stone. The center features two outdoor arenas, a wellness center, and trail access, offering boarding and diverse training disciplines to help horses develop physical and mental adaptability from trail riding to jumping.

name: Iron Oak Canyon Ranch
description: Iron Oak Canyon Ranch, formerly Bright Valley Farms, is an equestrian boarding facility in the scenic Sweetwater River Valley of Spring Valley, California. The property offers 24x24 and 24x48 corrals, lighted arenas, and exclusive trail access to the 11,000-acre San Diego National Wildlife Refuge, making it a unique destination for trail-focused horse owners.

name: Rancho Agave Equestrian
description: Rancho Agave Equestrian is a premier 60-acre facility in Valley Center, California, founded in 2022. The estate features a 50-stall barn, a jumping arena and dressage arena with Attwood Waterless Pinnacle footing, a 15-acre polo field, a 6-horse covered Eurociser, 30 individual paddocks, and a 60-foot covered round pen with GGT footing, offering top-level care for a select group of boarding clients.

name: Two Mountains Farm
description: A 105-acre equestrian property nestled at the base of Novelty Hill in Carnation, Washington. Two Mountains Farm offers full boarding and clinic programs focused on dressage and eventing in a comfortable, safe environment designed for horses to thrive physically, mentally, and socially. Facilities include a 100x200 ft covered arena, a 150x150 ft outdoor arena, 10-stall barn with runs, grass paddocks, and a heated wash rack.

name: Villa Fleur de Lys Stables
description: A highly rated horse boarding stable in Maple Valley, Washington, offering full-care boarding on a mud-free property with year-round dry conditions. The facility provides direct access to Taylor Mountain's thousands of acres of trails, an outdoor arena, a large round pen, and a hot/cold wash rack. Board includes timothy and/or alfalfa with a minimum of three daily feedings, herd turnout, and graveled stall runs.

name: Ravenswood Farm
description: A 25-acre multi-disciplinary equestrian facility in Issaquah, Washington, just 10 minutes from downtown Issaquah and under 30 minutes from Seattle. Ravenswood Farm features 41 12x12 stalls with rubber matting, a lighted outdoor arena with sport-LED lighting and ground rubber footing, a climate-controlled indoor arena with heated lounge and viewing room, and grass and dirt turnout. The facility welcomes all disciplines and skill levels, from beginners to advanced competitors.

name: Rockmeadow Equestrian Center
description: A renowned 30-year equestrian institution in Sammamish, Washington, dedicated to riders of all ages and abilities pursuing their passion for horses. Rockmeadow Equestrian Center is a premier hunter jumper facility offering training programs, riding school, summer camps, and school break programs. Boarding is available exclusively to riders enrolled in on-site training programs.

name: Western Sky Equestrian Center
description: A secure-gate western horsemanship facility in Redmond, Washington, just minutes from downtown Redmond and the Redmond Watershed trails. Western Sky Equestrian Center offers boarding, lessons, training, and clinics in a clean, well-maintained stable with a large covered arena, covered round pen, and access to extensive trail systems. The facility's 18-horse lesson program and emphasis on trust, communication, and feel make it an ideal destination for riders seeking a genuine western horsemanship experience.

name: The Equestrian School PNW
description: A classical horsemanship school located at Patterson Creek Farm in Fall City, Washington, serving the greater Seattle area with over 25 years of experience. The Equestrian School PNW operates under a Whole-Body, Whole-Herd philosophy, prioritizing the horse's physical, emotional, and mental well-being alongside student development. Programs include youth and adult riding lessons, a Mastery Riding Team, daily barn tours and intro lessons, and boarding for resident horses.

name: Sharkey Farm
description: A 15-acre hunter jumper training facility on Snoqualmie Ridge, just two miles from Snoqualmie Parkway and 30 minutes from Seattle. Sharkey Farm offers boarding, lessons, IEA team participation, and co-op boarding options under the guidance of trainer Kate Sharkey. The facility features a 150x72 ft all-weather outdoor arena with Equiloft footing, a heated tack room, and access to diverse riding experiences including beach rides, cross-country jumping, mountain trail rides, and urban trail rides.

name: Patterson Creek Equestrian Center
description: A 40-acre premier equestrian facility in Redmond, Washington, surrounded by miles of manicured trails. Patterson Creek Equestrian Center offers boarding, training, sales, and instruction for all levels of horses and riders, with dressage, jumping, and eventing as primary disciplines. The facility features large stalls, daily turnout, three feedings per day, a covered arena, a large outdoor jumping arena, and direct access to on-property trail networks.

name: The Stables at Magnolia Ridge
description: A full-care, multi-discipline equestrian facility set on a stunning 40-acre property in Monroe, Washington, with sweeping views of the Cascade Mountains and Tualco Valley. Located approximately 30 minutes from the greater Eastside, The Stables at Magnolia Ridge caters to show-jumping, dressage, and eventing riders at all competitive levels. The facility is operated by Giddyup Inc. and offers premium boarding care in a breathtaking natural setting.

name: Rosebud River Ranch
description: A premiere full-service boarding, breeding, and training facility just 30 miles from downtown Seattle along the I-90 corridor in Snoqualmie, Washington. Rosebud River Ranch features a 29-stall boarding barn with two tack rooms, a medical room, and a broodmare barn, plus a state-of-the-art 200x70 ft covered arena with premium footing. The ranch is home to Juliane Henry Equestrian, offering English riding lessons and dressage training, as well as corporate retreat experiences set against a stunning pastoral backdrop.

name: Sisterhood Stables
description: Sisterhood Stables is a unique equestrian experience catering to women entrepreneurs and leaders, as well as clients whose horses need extra care and love. Located on 14 acres in Columbus, NC, the 9-stall barn features a 50-ft round pen, wash rack with hot and cold water, FETA trail access, and a positive atmosphere where horse and rider can thrive together.

name: Renovatio Farm
description: Renovatio Farm is a full-service boarding and training facility on 50+ acres nestled in the foothills of the Blue Ridge Mountains, less than a mile from Tryon International Equestrian Center. Owner and trainer Trayce Doubek offers individualized care, dressage and jumping arenas, miles of trails, and training across dressage, hunter/jumper, and eventing disciplines. The farm features two barns with 20 matted stalls, heated wash racks, and climate-controlled tack rooms.

name: Cherokee Hill Farm
description: Cherokee Hill Farm is a full-service 100-acre horse farm in Tryon, NC, serving Polk, Spartanburg, and Greenville Counties for over 36 years. The main barn features 25 box stalls and a 200-foot center aisle with hot water wash racks, while the lower barn adds 11 more stalls. Facilities include a 250x125-ft hunter ring and a 1,000-ft outside course. Direct access to FENCE and the 140-mile FETA trail system. Specialties include equine rehabilitation, hunt seat instruction, and leasing school horses.

name: Foothills Equestrian Nature Center (FENCE)
description: The Foothills Equestrian Nature Center (FENCE) is one of the finest equestrian facilities in the Southeast, set on 384 protected acres in Tryon, NC. The equestrian center offers permanent stabling for over 300 horses across eleven barns, three lighted show rings including a covered arena with all-weather footing, and hosts events in all disciplines year-round. FENCE also operates TROT, a therapeutic riding program for individuals with physical and cognitive challenges.

name: Block House Sport Horses
description: Block House Sport Horses, operated by trainer Kelly Kocher at Finally Farm of Tryon, is a premier hunter/jumper training facility on 45 acres minutes from Tryon International Equestrian Center. The farm features a large all-weather outdoor arena with a full course of show jumps, heated and air-conditioned tack room, two wash racks with overhead heat, and an expert equine rehabilitation program using modalities including Theraplate, Horsegym treadmill, and Magna Wave. The program follows a comprehensive show schedule at TIEC and travels to Wellington, FL in winter.

name: Still Creek Equine
description: Still Creek Farm in Columbus, NC, is a professional equine rehabilitation facility offering the highest standard of care in a productive and stress-free environment. The experienced staff works to provide individualized rehabilitation programs for horses recovering from injury or illness, with a focus on quiet, attentive management in the scenic Polk County foothills.

name: Serenity Acres
description: Serenity Acres has been a trusted horse boarding facility in the NC foothills since 1994, offering individualized care for horses of all ages in a safe and fun environment. The property features an 110x210 outdoor dressage arena with jumps, 11 total box stalls across two barns with hot water, a heated tack room, and direct access to the CETA trail system. Full board includes twice-daily stall cleaning, individual turnout, daily fresh water, and comprehensive horse management.

name: Dream Haven Farm
description: Dream Haven Farm in Columbus, NC, is a welcoming equestrian facility offering horse boarding and summer equestrian camp programs in the scenic Polk County foothills. The farm provides a nurturing environment for horses and riders of all levels, combining quality care with a community-focused atmosphere in the heart of Tryon horse country near Tryon International Equestrian Center.

name: Cavallo Dreams Farm
description: A premium care, full service equestrian facility operating for 90+ years in Scarsdale, featuring the largest indoor ring in Westchester County. Offers lessons for all ages and levels, boarding, leasing, showing, summer camp, and party services with tailored programs for equine athletes and riders.

name: Trinity Equestrian Center
description: Located in Dutchess County near Germantown, Red Hook, Kingston, and Hudson, Trinity Equestrian Center is known for its well-trained riders and horses. The center provides a welcoming and well-equipped environment for riding, boarding, and training, with beginner riders of every age a specialty.

name: The Southlands Foundation
description: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit established in 1983 with barn facilities dating to 1938, situated on nearly 200 acres in Rhinebeck spanning approximately 90% conservation easement land managed with Scenic Hudson. Connecting people, nature, and horses through riding lessons for all ages, equine-assisted programs, and summer camps.

name: Juckas Riding Stables
description: Established in 1965, this family-owned facility features 117 acres of breathtaking farmland and trails through shaded woods, green fields, past lakes and streams in Pine Bush. Offering trail rides, summer camps for kids, after-school programs, birthday parties, and corporate events year-round with gentle, well-behaved horses and experienced guides.

name: Ellerslie Stable
description: A state-of-the-art equestrian facility on 650 acres with panoramic views of the Hudson River in Rhinebeck. Features a 100x200 indoor arena, 250x140 sand arena, 400x400 grass jump field, 25 matted box stalls, heated tack rooms, and miles of groomed trails — offering a world-class environment for all levels of horse and rider.

name: Old Salem Farm
description: A premier hunter-jumper facility on 120 acres in North Salem, Westchester County, featuring over 60 stalls, 2 indoor arenas, 4 outdoor riding rings, a Grand Prix field, boarders' lounge with gym, and spacious paddocks. Horses live in the lap of luxury at this state-of-the-art facility offering top-level training and the highest quality of care.

name: Echo Farm
description: A bustling equestrian facility established in 1826 in South Salem, bordering the Ward Pound Ridge Reservation trails. USHJA approved, offering riding lessons for children and adults, an IEA team, horsemanship program, and boarding. Features English and Western riding, dressage, and a recently renovated enclosed jump ring.

name: Netherwood Acres
description: A full-service equestrian facility offering horseback riding, horse boarding, training, and sales in Hyde Park, Dutchess County. Set in the scenic Hudson Valley, Netherwood Acres provides personalized care in a rural farm setting.

name: Hazelnut Farms
description: An equestrian farm in South Salem, Westchester County, offering boarding and riding programs. Located on scenic grounds in the heart of Westchester horse country, Hazelnut Farms provides quality care in a peaceful rural setting.

name: Larkspur Farm
description: A top-quality full service boarding facility located in North Salem, NY, with exceptional design and state-of-the-art amenities built for efficiency, safety, and wellbeing. Offering lessons, boarding, training, showing, sales, and leasing with year-round programs and winter travel to Ocala, FL for competitions.

name: Centurion Farm
description: Centurion Farm is a premier equestrian facility nestled in the heart of Alpharetta, GA offering boarding and riding lessons for riders at all stages. Focused on hunter/jumper equitation, the farm provides expert instruction in a supportive environment with individualized attention to help riders achieve their goals from local schooling shows to AA competitions.

name: Windsor Stables
description: Windsor Stables is Atlanta's premiere equestrian center, a full-service boarding and training facility dedicated to the goal-oriented rider. Their world-class trainers and grooms cater to each client's specific needs, offering top-quality horse care and instruction in and out of the arena. Windsor operates a competitive show team achieving championships in junior hunters, equitation medals, and jumpers throughout the Southeast and Florida, plus a riding academy program designed for all experience levels including working adults.

name: In Your Dreams Horse Farm
description: In Your Dreams Horse Farm is a professional 22-acre training venue in Alpharetta offering dry stalls, two spacious arenas (a large covered arena and an outdoor lighted arena), and a collaborative community for trainers dedicated to excellence. The facility is home to multiple independent trainers and emphasizes flexibility and support for equestrian entrepreneurs. The farm supports dressage programming under the Dressage Alpharetta initiative and provides stall leasing to professional trainers.

name: Chastain Horse Park
description: Chastain Horse Park is one of the last two remaining urban horse parks in the United States, located within Atlanta's Chastain Park community. As a 501(c)(3) non-profit since 1999, it is home to a nationally recognized PATH-accredited therapeutic horsemanship program, a general boarding program, a school riding program, and event space. The facility empowers individuals of all abilities through life-changing relationships with horses, serving riders from beginners through advanced students and offering therapeutic hippotherapy and equine-assisted learning programs.

name: Fortitude Farm
description: Fortitude Farm is a premier hunter/jumper training, boarding, and lesson facility in the Alpharetta/Milton area north of Atlanta. Founded by Carolyn Bell, the farm offers instruction at all levels with a mission to help riders achieve individual goals through friendly, positive instruction. Riders compete at AA shows throughout the Southeast and Florida, consistently winning zone and state year-end awards. The farm emphasizes character development in both horse and rider through goal-setting and achievement, with school horses available for beginners.

name: Millstone Stables
description: Millstone Stables is a first-class hunter/jumper boarding, training, and lesson facility set on 16 rolling acres in north metro Atlanta, centrally located on the border of north Fulton and southeast Cherokee counties convenient to Woodstock, Milton, Alpharetta, Roswell, East Cobb, and Canton. The Morton-built 12-stall barn features 12x12 stalls with individual fans, tongue-and-groove walls, double Dutch doors, rubber mat flooring, and a centrally monitored fire alarm and video system. Outdoor amenities include a lighted 100x200 foot fully irrigated arena with show-quality jumps, nine half-acre paddocks, a hot/cold water wash bay, and perimeter trails.

name: WildeWood Farm
description: WildeWood Farm is a privately-owned, multi-generational farm in Cumming, Georgia, operating since 1996. The farm has been voted Best in Forsyth five consecutive years for best stable and best equestrian facility. They offer riding lessons for all ages and skill levels, summer day camps (Horse Camp, Farm Adventure Camp, Hybrid Camp), birthday parties, therapeutic horsemanship for at-risk youth, equine-assisted learning for corporate leadership training, and educational programs. Run by three generations of family farmers, WildeWood Farm creates transformative experiences for youth and adults alike.

name: Jaid Stables
description: Jaid Stables is Canton's premier stables for horse boarding and rider training, nationally recognized as a training academy that gives clients the correct building blocks for a proven lifetime of successful horse showing. The facility offers three comprehensive boarding packages with custom feeding, daily turnout, stall cleaning, and night checks, plus optional add-on services including show prep grooming, body clipping, tack cleaning, and laundry. Their training programs are tailored to each horse and rider pair and include a minimum of four training units per month integrated with boarding packages.

name: Lioncrest Farms
description: Lioncrest Farms is a full-service equestrian facility in Canton, Georgia offering boarding, riding lessons, horse training, hippotherapy, and summer camp. The facility features twenty-two 12x12 matted stalls with fans, two large tack rooms, two wash racks, a lighted indoor arena with sand footing dragged daily, an outdoor all-weather jumping arena, and a heated and cooled lounge with kitchen, WiFi, and viewing area. Full board starts at $900 per month with daily turnout and twice-daily feeding. Lesson fees start at $65 for beginners through advanced riders.

name: The Horse Park at Woodside
description: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit equestrian facility on 362 acres near Woodside, California. The Horse Park at Woodside hosts premier competition events including the Woodside Horse Trials, and offers boarding, training, a covered arena, cross-country course, and educational programs. Built around four mission pillars: horse sport, land stewardship, lifelong learning, and community engagement.

name: Williams Ranch Boarding
description: A historic 150-acre family ranch in the Santa Cruz Mountains just 15 minutes from downtown Woodside, owned and operated by the Williams/McReynolds family since 1853. The property offers gelding-only boarding with 12x12 and 12x14 stalls, spacious single-horse paddocks with shelters, daily pasture turnout, a 12,788 sq ft outdoor sand arena, pastures ranging from 7 to over 50 acres, and indoor hot and cold water wash rack.

name: Oasis Farms Woodside
description: A premier hunter/jumper and dressage training facility located at Portola Farms in Woodside, serving the greater Bay Area. Trainers Janeen Smith and Colin Daly bring over 30 combined years of experience, offering lessons, competitive training, and an IEA (Interscholastic Equestrian Association) program. The facility emphasizes horsemanship, horse training, and building lasting partnerships between horse and rider.

name: Windy Hill Equestrians
description: A premier Bay Area hunter/jumper facility located at Fremont Hills Country Club in Los Altos Hills, conveniently situated minutes off Highway 280. Windy Hill offers an all-inclusive horse and rider training program with individualized attention, riding school, training program, lessons, and summer horsemanship camps. The facility serves clients from San Francisco to South San Jose.

name: Garrod Farms Stables
description: A family-owned estate winery and equestrian center in Saratoga since 1893, offering both western and English riding instruction alongside private horse boarding. Garrod Farms features five riding arenas (one covered), two longing arenas, a round pen, three horse wash racks, and access to over twenty miles of trails through the Santa Cruz Mountains. All instructors are CHA-certified.

name: Miwok Equestrian Center
description: Formerly Miwok Livery Stables, Miwok Equestrian Center is a nonprofit organization set within the Golden Gate National Recreation Area in Tennessee Valley, Mill Valley. The center offers English hunter/jumper lessons for riders ages 6 and up, show teams, and seasonal summer camps. The facility is surrounded by over 1,000 acres of parkland with expansive trail riding opportunities and maintains a boarding waitlist.

name: Dickson Ranch
description: A six-generation family-owned horse facility in beautiful Woodacre, West Marin County, offering boarding, lessons, and multi-discipline use arenas. The property features an indoor arena, two outdoor arenas (White and Red), a round pen, and cross-country trails through open fields and redwood forest. Boarders enjoy 12x12 stalls with run-in areas and paddocks starting at $650/month.

name: Longview Stables
description: A full-service training program in Novato dedicated to teaching all riders from their first pony ride through advanced competition. Longview Stables emphasizes personal development and sportsmanship alongside riding skills, with programs spanning beginner through competitive levels. The facility's training philosophy focuses on building well-rounded equestrians.

name: Ocean Riders of Marin
description: A sustainably run, community-based nonprofit horse boarding facility at the historic Golden Gate Dairy Stables in Muir Beach, operating as a Park Partner with the Golden Gate National Recreation Area. Horses live in roomy paddocks with attached shelters and receive daily turnout. The facility offers access to spectacular coastal trails looping through redwood forests and along the Pacific coast.

name: Blue Dot Barn
description: A full-service horse boarding facility nestled in the scenic Nicasio Valley of Marin County, California. Blue Dot Barn is committed to providing exceptional care for horses in a beautiful natural setting, offering a peaceful environment with spacious accommodations and dedicated on-site management. The Nicasio Valley location provides easy access to West Marin's expansive trail network.

name: Walnut Hill Stables
description: Walnut Hill Stables is a family-owned equestrian facility in Marengo, Ohio, conveniently located just 2 miles off I-71 and 30 minutes from downtown Columbus. The facility offers boarding, riding lessons, training, sales, and summer camps. The program specializes in dressage, jumping, eventing, English pleasure, and western disciplines for all experience levels, and fields both a Hunt Seat and Dressage IEA team.

name: Lindsay Yinger Show Stables
description: Lindsay Yinger Show Stables specializes in cultivating riders' careers from lead line through 'AA' shows, offering tailored hunter/jumper programs since 2006. Located in Croton, Ohio, the facility has produced regional, zone, and national champions, and has coached the Otterbein University equestrian team since 2010. Programs include lessons, summer camps, horse sales, show preparation, and comprehensive equine care guidance.

name: TAG Farms
description: TAG Farms is the premier equine facility in Central Ohio, located at 8363 Schleppi Road in Westerville, just 5 minutes from Highway 161 and convenient to New Albany, Gahanna, and Johnstown. The facility offers English and Western riding lessons for beginner through advanced students, horse boarding (waitlist basis), and hosts equestrian events. TAG Farms is situated in Northeast Franklin County between Westerville and New Albany.

name: Colter Equestrian Center
description: Colter Equestrian Center is a hunter/jumper barn located near Columbus, Ohio in Plain City. Under trainer Carrie McCurrach, Colter offers horseback riding lessons for all levels from beginner through advanced, as well as training and boarding. The facility specializes in hunter/jumper horses and provides a welcoming environment for riders looking to develop their skills in the hunter/jumper discipline.

name: Duzan Riding Academy
description: Founded in 2009, Duzan Riding Academy is a USHJA Recognized Riding Academy on 23 acres in Ostrander, Ohio. The facility features a 200' x 90' insulated indoor arena, a 250' x 125' outdoor arena, quality constructed stalls, trails, and a jump field. Duzan offers comprehensive programs for all ages and skill levels, including hunter/jumper, huntseat equitation, dressage, and cross-country, as well as US Pony Club membership, schooling shows, and summer/winter camps.

name: Wheeler Stables
description: Wheeler Stables is an 18-acre horse farm in Westerville, Ohio offering a friendly, laid-back environment for horses and riders. The facility features 16 spacious 12x12 stalls, 5 large pastures, 3 dry lots, indoor and outdoor riding arenas, a large tack room, and a hot and cold wash rack. Full board includes daily stall mucking, grain and hay, deworming, and maximized turnout. English riding lessons are available for all levels.

name: Lunar Winds Stables
description: Lunar Winds Stables is a family-run equestrian facility at 4890 Neff Road in Grove City, Ohio. The main barn offers 25 stalls with skylights and a wide aisle, plus a separate indoor barrel racing track with 30 additional stalls. The facility features a 145' x 160' outdoor arena, a 45' x 60' indoor arena, 6 paddocks, and 2 large pastures. Disciplines include western (with a barrel racing focus) and English jumping. Open daily 8am to 10pm.

name: Creekside Equestrian
description: Creekside Equestrian is a full-service, family-owned boarding and training facility located in Parker, Colorado. The horse-first philosophy ensures each animal receives individualized care and attention, with a welcoming community atmosphere for riders of all levels.

name: Bell Mountain Ranch Equestrian Center
description: Bell Mountain Ranch Equestrian Center in Castle Rock combines state-of-the-art technology with personalized care across multiple boarding options. The facility features a large indoor arena, two outdoor arenas, and over 30 miles of trails, welcoming all riding styles in a no-drama environment.

name: Black Kettle Ranch
description: Black Kettle Ranch in Parker is owned and operated by professional trainer Tina Huston, who brings over 35 years of experience in natural horsemanship, classical dressage, and Western dressage. The facility offers boarding, lessons, training, breeding, and clinics for riders and horses of all ages and levels.

name: Mariah Farms
description: Located in historic Castle Rock, Colorado, Mariah Farms is a full-care boarding facility catering to English, Dressage, Western Dressage, and Jumping disciplines. The 35-acre property features a 70x250 foot mirrored indoor arena, outdoor arena, round pen, and quality hay and grain included with every stall.

name: Platinum Farms
description: Platinum Farms is a beautiful, privately owned full-service horse boarding and training facility in Franktown, Colorado, 35 miles southeast of Denver. Specializing in eventing, dressage, and jumping, the facility features an on-site cross country course and hosts schooling shows and clinics throughout the season.

name: Cottonwood Riding Club
description: Cottonwood Riding Club is a 100-acre equestrian facility adjacent to Chatfield State Park in Littleton, Colorado, just 30 minutes from downtown Denver. The family-owned facility offers boarding, riding lessons, and professional training across Western and English disciplines, with direct access to miles of scenic trails through Roxborough and Chatfield state parks.

name: Coventry Farms
description: Coventry Farms is one of Colorado's leading hunter/jumper barns, located in Littleton just 20 minutes from Downtown Denver. The facility offers top-tier riding lessons, training, and boarding with a welcoming community where clients become family, serving both beginners and seasoned competitors.

name: Mile High Horse Ranch
description: Established in 1976 and located southeast of Denver near Parker, Mile High Horse Ranch is a premier eventing facility with a licensed cross country course featuring 20-22 fences at every level from Elementary through Preliminary. The ranch offers boarding, dressage, stadium jumping, and cross-country training with a 3/4-mile gallop lane and 2 miles of adjacent trails.

name: Stockton's Plum Creek Stables
description: Stockton's Plum Creek Stables has been serving the Littleton, Colorado equestrian community for over five decades, backing directly onto Chatfield State Park for exclusive trail access. The facility offers boarding, lessons across Western, English, trail, and dressage disciplines, as well as a lighted indoor arena, hayrides, and western events.

name: Dead Broke Farm
description: A 110-acre working horse farm in Raleigh offering pasture-only boarding with more than 10 miles of wooded trails featuring creek crossings and rolling hills. Horses receive grain twice daily, hay from an on-site hay hut, and access to a warm-water wash station and private tack room. Affordable rates for light saddle horses, drafts, and ponies make this a top choice for trail-focused boarders in the Triangle.

name: Triton Stables
description: A family-owned hunter/jumper facility with over 40 years of history, situated on 59 acres just 10 miles from North Raleigh and Brier Creek. Two 22-stall barns totaling 44 stalls are complemented by three all-weather show-regulation riding rings, 16 pastures, and robust programming including IEA teams, summer camps, horse training, and leasing. A premier destination for competitive hunter/jumper riders of all levels.

name: MacNair's Country Acres
description: A full-service equestrian center in southwest Raleigh offering horse boarding, leases, riding lessons for all ages, IEA and 4-H programs, horse shows, and popular summer horseback riding camps. The facility's welcoming environment and diverse programming make it a community hub for young equestrians and families in the greater Raleigh area.

name: Brightleaf Stables
description: A beginner-friendly boarding and lesson barn in Durham offering horseback riding instruction for all ages and skill levels. Known for its gentle, patient horses that serve as ideal teachers for novice riders, Brightleaf Stables also hosts birthday parties, pony events, summer camps, and provides horse boarding. Located conveniently on Sherron Road near the Research Triangle area.

name: Chapel Hill Equestrian
description: A full-service horse boarding facility conveniently located in Chapel Hill and accessible to Durham and Hillsborough. Chapel Hill Equestrian welcomes boarders of all riding disciplines and allows outside trainers to work on-site at no extra charge, making it an unusually flexible and inclusive barn. Contact Susan Waters or Adnan Wasiudiin for boarding availability and facility tours.

name: Castle Farm
description: A 12-acre working barn in Knightdale established in 1999, offering horseback riding lessons, boarding, and training for all skill levels. Headed by owner and trainer Heather King, Castle Farm operates as a true working barn and requires appointments for visits. The intimate setting is well-suited for riders seeking personalized instruction and a close-knit equestrian community just east of Raleigh.

name: Apex Equestrian Center
description: A dedicated hunter/jumper facility in Apex focused on developing quality jumper horses and skilled riders. Apex Equestrian Center offers professional training programs, boarding, and lessons on Green Level Road in the heart of Wake County. The facility is committed to producing competitive riders and well-trained horses for the show ring.

name: Grace Lee Farm
description: A horse riding lesson facility in Apex, NC, offering instruction for riders of all ages and experience levels. Situated on Olive Chapel Road in the heart of western Wake County, Grace Lee Farm provides a welcoming environment for students looking to develop their horsemanship skills in a structured, caring setting near Cary and Apex.

name: Firstview Farm Dressage
description: A boutique dressage training and boarding facility in Apex, NC, dedicated to the art of classical dressage. Firstview Farm provides a focused, professional environment for dressage riders at all levels, from introductory through Grand Prix work. The farm's Paddock Drive location in western Wake County offers convenient access to the Triangle's equestrian community.

name: Sunrise Community Farm Center
description: A community-oriented equestrian and agricultural center in Chapel Hill offering therapeutic and recreational horseback riding programs for riders of all abilities. Located on Millhouse Road, Sunrise Community Farm Center serves the Chapel Hill and Carrboro communities with programs designed to make equestrian activities accessible and enriching for people of all backgrounds and needs.

name: Oakwood Farms Equestrian
description: Premier horse boarding barn serving Barrington Hills and the greater Illinois area since 1996. With 60 stalls, all-day pasture access, paddock turnout, all-weather paddocks, and 24/7 on-site management, Oakwood Farms is dedicated to the passion, tradition, and legacy of the equestrian lifestyle. Adjacent to the Cook County Forest Preserve.

name: Old Barrington Farm
description: Premier Midwest equestrian center set on over 60 acres in North Barrington, a northwest suburb of Chicago. Offering boarding and training for hunters and jumpers from short stirrup through Grand Prix level in a fun, nurturing team environment, with a focus on the long-term well-being of the horse. Features a heated indoor ring, spacious outdoor ring, grass field, and all-weather paddocks with trail access.

name: Laura's Ark
description: A small, private 15-stall barn with a heated indoor arena located in the heart of horse country in Barrington Hills. Laura's Ark specializes in classical dressage instruction and foundational horsemanship training, offering a boutique environment focused on individual horse and rider development.

name: Deerwood Farm
description: Deerwood Farm is an equestrian facility located 30 miles northwest of Chicago, specializing in hunters, equitation, jumpers, and ponies. Situated at 99 West County Line Road in scenic Barrington Hills, the farm provides a welcoming environment for riders of all levels with private and group lesson programs.

name: Campton Hills Equestrian
description: Campton Hills Equestrian, LLC operates out of McCarthy Farm, a premium boarding facility in Elgin, Illinois. A family-owned facility offering horse boarding, sales, lessons, shows, birthday parties, and summer camps. Services include basic board ($640/mo) and premium board ($740/mo), private lessons ($55 for 45 min, or 4-pack for $200), and training programs for all skill levels from beginner to advanced.

name: Lakewood Stables
description: Lakewood Farm & Stables LLC in Wauconda offers multi-discipline equestrian boarding with complete and custom packages including boarding, horse leasing, share boarding, and lessons. Specializing in hunter/jumper with all-day turnout, quality hay and grain fed twice daily, stalls cleaned 7 days a week, and blanketing service included with premium board.

name: Dunham Woods Farm
description: Superior hunter-jumper barn in northern Illinois, situated on 20+ acres in Elgin. Led by Michele, Rose, and a team with over 50 years of combined experience developing students from beginner to Olympic caliber. Amenities include a 65'x150' indoor arena with rubber cushion footing, a 100'x180' sand outdoor arena, a 10+ acre riding field, heated indoor wash rack, outdoor wash racks, matted groom stalls, and all-weather dry-lot paddocks.

name: Galway Farm
description: Established in 1998, Galway Farm Hunters & Jumpers is conveniently located 15 miles northwest of O'Hare International Airport in Long Grove, Illinois. Offering a limited number of boarding stalls for hunter and jumper horses, full training programs, lesson programs for all levels, on-site clinics, full-service grooming, and horse transport. Led by trainer Lorrie Canady.

name: Messenger Hill Farm
description: World-class hunter-jumper training and boarding facility in Mettawa, Illinois (near Libertyville), with a second location in Wellington, FL. Offering Olympic-sized all-weather jump arenas, heated indoor arenas, hot walkers, treadmills, and a riding academy with full training programs. Led by trainers Jodi and Freddie Vazquez, who travel extensively with the A-Show team to prestigious events in North America.

name: Millcreek Equestrian
description: Full-service hunter-jumper boarding and training facility in Antioch, Illinois, offering stalls cleaned daily, all-day turnout year-round, blanketing, three meals per day, and supplement administration. Features a 52'x160' indoor arena, a 95'x225' outdoor arena, a grass jumping field, on-property trails, a hot walker, automatic fly spray, and heated 14x14 matted stalls. Trainer Serah Vogus brings training under Olympian Carlos Milthaler.

name: Shamrock Grove Arabians
description: Full-service, all-inclusive horse boarding facility in Lee's Summit, MO featuring an 80'x160' indoor arena with halogen lighting, seven large turnout lots with heated water tanks, climate-controlled tack room, and a welcoming environment for riders of all ages and disciplines.

name: Sunset Trails Stables
description: Family-focused horse boarding and lesson facility in Lee's Summit, MO situated on 26 acres adjacent to 2,700-acre James A. Reed Memorial Wildlife Area with miles of riding trails. Features two barns, a 120'x60' indoor arena, two outdoor arenas, wash rack, and a fun community atmosphere.

name: Black Diamond Ranch
description: Family-owned equestrian center on 25 acres in north Kansas City, MO offering full-service and self-care pasture boarding. The facility features stalls, paddocks, a full-sized outdoor arena, round pen, wash bays, tack room, and large pastures with run-in sheds overseen by a rancher with 50+ years of experience.

name: Black Oak Ranch
description: Premier 17-acre horse boarding facility and event venue in Lone Jack, MO just outside Lee's Summit, owned by barrel horse professional Charles Wilbers. Features 15 oversized insulated stalls in a show barn, a 100'x150' indoor arena, a 125'x300' outdoor arena with overhead lighting, two tack rooms, wash bay, and three turnout paddocks.

name: Woodson Hill Equestrian Center
description: Full-service boarding facility on ten acres in suburban northwest Kansas City, MO offering standard stall board with daily cleaning, grain, hay twice daily, heated water in winter, automatic fly spray, and daily turnout. Also provides riding instruction, horse training, summer camps, and clinics.

name: SaddleCreek Stables & Vineyard
description: Private country club equestrian facility in southeast Kansas City, MO offering boarding, trail rides, riding lessons, vineyard, and venue rental. Boarding options include full-care barn stall, self-care stall, and paddock with individual or group turnout. Riding lessons available for ages 5 to adult.

name: Liberty Farm Equestrian Center
description: A horse lover's paradise in Liberty, MO specializing in American Saddlebred show horses. Full-care equestrian center with 44 stalls, indoor and outdoor arenas, paddock and pasture, 4 grooming stalls, two wash stalls, and tack rooms. Boarding, training, lessons, and horse sales available for all ages and experience levels.

trainers.name: Jeff Tryon
trainers.bio: World champion American Saddlebred trainer who has started approximately 14 World Champion show horses.

name: Misty River Equestrian Center
description: Well-planned 55-acre equestrian facility in eastern Independence, MO nestled in a rich valley at I-70 and Blue River Parkway. Features 50 stalls, two indoor arenas, a 250'x125' outdoor arena, trails, round pen, warm water wash bay, and automatic fly spray system in a safe, family-oriented environment.

name: Landers Dressage
description: Small private dressage facility in Liberty, MO owned by Linda Landers, who has been active in the Kansas City dressage community for over 40 years. Provides ongoing educational opportunities for horses and riders, clinics, events, and sales horses with a focus on classical dressage.

trainers.name: Linda Landers
trainers.bio: Active in the Kansas City dressage community for over 40 years, specializing in classical dressage education for horses and riders.

name: Wood's Edge Equestrian Center
description: Multi-functional equestrian facility on 40 scenic acres in south Johnson County, Olathe, KS offering hunter/jumper lessons and boarding. Features 22 stalls, an 80'x180' indoor arena, a 100'x200' outdoor arena, scenic wooded trail, individual turnout paddocks, and a temperature-controlled lounge. Also home to the REACH therapeutic riding program.

trainers.name: Katy Harcsar
trainers.bio: Owner of Wood's Edge Equestrian Center, specializing in hunter/jumper instruction for riders ages 5 and up.

name: Keene Ridge Farm
description: Keene Ridge Farm is nestled in the heart of Central Kentucky on 170 rolling acres near Keeneland off Bosworth Rd. A thoroughbred nursery farm turned full-service boarding and training facility, Keene Ridge offers riding lessons for all ages led by expert instructors. Located just down the street from Keeneland Race Course, the farm provides a tranquil and professional boarding experience for horses of all breeds and disciplines, with a focus on quality equine care and rider development through workshops and clinics.

name: Stone Columns Stables
description: Stone Columns Stables is a premier equestrian facility operating across three farms spanning nearly 1,000 acres in the heart of Lexington, KY, just 5 minutes from the Kentucky Horse Park on Iron Works Pike and Paris Pike. Devoted to breeding top-quality sport horses for the dressage and jumper arenas, the facility offers premier horse boarding and retirement care on 300 acres of the best fields in Central Kentucky. With a client mare breeding program and young horse development program, Stone Columns is a destination for serious dressage and sport horse enthusiasts.

name: Three Crowns Farm
description: Three Crowns Farm is a welcoming, family-friendly boarding and lesson facility located in Lexington, KY, close to the University of Kentucky and the Kentucky Horse Park. Offering pasture and stall boarding for horses of all breeds, ages, and disciplines, Three Crowns is known for its encouraging atmosphere for riders ages 3 to 83. The farm provides riding lessons from beginner to advanced, summer camps full of hands-on activities, equine layover/horse hotel services, and event hosting for weddings, birthday parties, and field trips.

name: Bittersweet Acres Farm
description: Bittersweet Acres Farm is an established equine boarding facility on Military Pike in Lexington, KY. The farm features 35 matted 12x12 stalls across two barns, both with tack areas and wash racks with hot water. Riders enjoy a 220x120 outdoor jumping arena and a 180x80 covered indoor riding arena with premium GGT footing, plus a cross-country jump course and over 50 acres for trail riding. With flexible boarding packages from self-care to partial care, the farm welcomes Thoroughbreds, Warmbloods, Arabians, Quarter Horses, and more.

name: Cloud Nine Farm
description: Cloud Nine Farm is a full-service hunter/jumper training and boarding facility in Midway, KY, just 20 minutes from Downtown Lexington, the University of Kentucky, and the Kentucky Horse Park. Owner and head trainer Stevie McCarron leads the program with a focus on quality over quantity, providing individualized care for equine athletes and developing competitive riders at all levels. The farm offers an indoor and outdoor arena, participates in KHJA competitions, and fosters a supportive, goal-oriented community of equestrians.

name: Three Day Farm
description: Three Day Farm is an active training and competition facility located in the heart of the Bluegrass in Midway, KY, situated on 45 acres with access to several hundred additional acres. Under head trainer Tanya Davis, the farm focuses primarily on Eventing while also offering classical dressage instruction. The facility includes a covered open-air indoor arena, cross-country courses, and hosts Transylvania University's equestrian team. Boarding options include full care in the main barn and pasture board in large fields with shelters and automatic waterers.

name: Graystone Stable
description: Graystone Stable is a small, private full-care boarding and training facility in Georgetown, KY, located on Ironworks Road about 10 minutes from the Kentucky Horse Park and Hagyard Equine Hospital. Specializing in dressage, jumpers, and eventing, the facility has served the Bluegrass equestrian community for over 18 years. Head trainer Stephanie Calendrillo offers lessons from beginner through intermediate levels and can assist with starting and retraining young horses. The stable operates across two farm locations totaling over 110 acres.

name: Scheffelridge Farm
description: Scheffelridge Farm is a family-oriented, multi-faceted equestrian venue in Paris, KY, that has been in business for over two decades. Convenient to both Georgetown and Lexington, the 120-acre farm is just 14 miles from the Kentucky Horse Park. The facility features a lighted 100x200 all-weather outdoor arena, an indoor arena, a round pen, and a full cross-country course with jumps up to Novice height. Instruction focuses on hunter, jumper, equitation, and dressage. The farm is also home to the Powerhouse Equestrian IEA Team and the Georgetown College Equestrian Team.

name: Punchestown Stable
description: Punchestown Stable is a premier hunter and jumper stable nestled in the heart of Bluegrass Country, serving Lexington's competitive equestrian community since 2002. Located on a 30-acre facility with live-in management, the barn offers a 270x150 outdoor arena with Martin Collins footing, a 150x80 indoor arena, 3 barns, 9 one-acre paddocks, and 3 five-acre fields with run-in sheds. Owner and head trainer Sarah Meier runs a comprehensive program including showing on USEF circuits, training, and lessons for all ages from Short Stirrup to Junior Jumpers.

name: Hester Equestrian
description: Hester Equestrian is the home of grand prix show jumper Lauren Hester, located in Lexington, KY just minutes from the Kentucky Horse Park and convenient to Tryon International Equestrian Center and the World Equestrian Center. Lauren actively competes at the Grand Prix level while training a select group of show clients. Having worked with Emile and Paul Hendrix in Europe, Hester Equestrian maintains a selection of quality sport horses for sale in the US and Europe. The facility caters to dedicated hunter, jumper, and equitation riders pursuing serious show goals.

name: Pine Crest Farm
description: Pine Crest Farm is a premier horse boarding and riding school established in 2014, situated on 40 acres within the Deep Run Hunt Club property in Manakin-Sabot. The farm features 25 stalls, multiple tack rooms, an office, and a lighted all-weather outdoor riding arena along with grass hacking fields.

name: James River Equestrian Center
description: James River Equestrian Center is a full-care facility located on the historic Keswick Plantation in Midlothian, offering English horseback riding lessons, boarding, and training. The 22-stall facility averages around 35 horses and specializes in hunter/jumper and English riding disciplines.

name: Blue Ridge Stables
description: Blue Ridge Stables in Ashland, Virginia emphasizes real horsemanship and welcomes all disciplines. The facility features a new indoor arena and outdoor arena, offering horse boarding, day care, riding lessons, clinics, coaching, and horse shows. All trainers, farriers, and vets are welcome on property.

name: Manakin-Sabot Equestrian Center
description: Manakin-Sabot Equestrian Center is a full-service equestrian facility and nonprofit horse retirement sanctuary located in Goochland County. Offering lessons for ages 4 to 74 in hunt seat equitation and basic dressage, the center features indoor and outdoor riding rings, grooming stalls, and a wash stall with hot and cold water.

name: Diamond Ridge Stables
description: Diamond Ridge Stables is a premium equestrian facility in Chesterfield, Virginia specializing in hunter, jumper, and quality sale horses. The 12-stall barn features rubber-matted stalls, 9 pastures with run-in shelters, a 75-foot round pen, wash stall, lighted outdoor ring, and trail access to Pocahontas State Park.

name: Oakdale Equestrian Center
description: Oakdale Equestrian Center is Powhatan's premier full-care boarding facility offering miles of trails, numerous arenas, a full cross-country course, an indoor ring, outdoor dressage ring with rubber footing, a round pen, and 130 acres of trails. They host horse trials and welcome dressage clinics, cross-country jumping, and all disciplines.

name: South Meadow Stables
description: South Meadow Stables is a small family-owned horse boarding facility in Ashland, Virginia with over 25 years of horse care experience. Set on 14 acres with 3 barns (6 total 12x12 stalls), a lit outdoor arena with all-weather footing, and 7 acres of pasture, they welcome all disciplines including dressage, English, western, and barrel racing.

name: Redbird Equestrian
description: Redbird Equestrian is an inclusive equestrian community in Chesterfield County offering training board, riding lessons, youth camps, trail rides, and horse sales. Situated at Keystone Acres with access to 800+ acres of trails, the facility features covered and open riding rings, show rings, and a qualified team of instructors led by Ingrid Patel.

name: Brandywine Farm
description: Brandywine Farm is a 75-acre equestrian facility in Chesterfield County owned by Ariel and Hunter Kinch, offering hunter/jumper and equitation lessons, pony rides, trail rides, and boarding. The farm features two lighted outdoor rings, an indoor arena, a cross-country course, and a 4-stall main barn with feed and tack rooms.

name: Hunters Ridge Equestrian Center
description: Hunters Ridge Equestrian Center is a family-friendly 20-acre facility in the heart of Powhatan, Virginia welcoming riders from all disciplines. The farm features 12+ stalls, multiple pastures with run-in sheds, a tack room, and an office. Internationally known instructors offer lessons to riders of all levels.

name: Sauvie Island Stables
description: A picturesque equine facility just minutes from downtown Portland, offering full-care boarding on 30 acres of hay fields with well-maintained arenas, spacious pastures, trail riding on 70+ acres, kids' camps, and equestrian photo sessions. Offers English and Western lessons for all ages and skill levels.

name: Sky Ranch Stables
description: A 250-acre equestrian facility in North Plains with over 30 miles of on-site trails, bright LED indoor arena (50x200), seasonal outdoor arena, 40-foot round pen, and hilltop views. Offers full-care boarding with daily turnout, premium hay, and a welcoming community for trail, western, and English riding. Approximately 25 minutes from Portland.

name: Sherwood Forest Equestrian Center
description: One of Oregon's largest and finest equestrian facilities on 120 acres in Sherwood, 17 miles south of downtown Portland. Features 52 stalls across 3 barns, a 100x220 indoor arena, 150x300 outdoor arena, and over 5 miles of groomed all-weather trails with a cross-country schooling course. Home to Red Horse Stables, a full-service Hunter/Jumper show barn.

name: French Hill Farm
description: A premier riding academy in the Charbonneau District of Aurora, open since 1997. Situated on 42 acres with a 52-stall barn, indoor and outdoor arenas, and an automated exerciser. Specializes in English riding and hunter jumper instruction for students age 7 and up through beginner to advanced levels. One of the foremost riding lesson programs in the Portland-Salem area.

name: Savin Hill Farm
description: A private sport horse boarding and training facility on 11 acres in the Chehalem Mountain Wine Growers area of Newberg, specializing in Hunters, Jumpers, and Eventing. Features an 18-stall barn with runs, indoor and outdoor arenas, and daily turnout. Head trainer Sarah Savin holds a Master's degree in mental health counseling and brings competition experience from Texas, Colorado, Australia, and Oregon.

name: Herrold Stables
description: A full-care boarding, training, and lesson facility in Hillsboro operated by Dan and Elaine Herrold. Features a 60x120 indoor arena with Nike footing, a 100x200 all-weather outdoor arena, a 60-foot sand round pen, 12x12 matted stalls with 42-foot runs, and 2 to 5 acre year-round pasture turnout. Offers English, Western, dressage, jumping, and harness driving disciplines for adults and youth.

name: Wilsonville Equestrian Center
description: A serene 23-acre equestrian facility in Tualatin with over 50 years of history, bordered by Boeckman Creek and the historic Frog Pond Grange district. Features a newly renovated barn with private half-acre pastures for each stall, covered and open arenas, and state-of-the-art footing. Specializes in hunter/jumper instruction with lessons for all ages and levels, plus boarding, summer camps, and horse camps.

name: Charlton Ridge Farm
description: A vibrant equestrian community in Sherwood offering individualized instruction, coaching, and comprehensive boarding and lease options. Owned and operated by trainer Kaitlyn Eigner. Offers an IEA team open to all grades 4-12, with lesson horses available so horse ownership is not required. Lesson packages and programs designed for riders of all levels.

name: Westside Stables
description: A meticulously maintained multi-discipline facility in Newberg offering high-quality lesson programs for ages 6 and up. Features an indoor riding arena, oversized matted box stalls with some attached runs, round pen, individual paddocks and group grass fields for daily turnout, and gated entry with video surveillance. Specializes in dressage, jumping, and eventing with a manager with over 20 years of sport horse experience on site.

name: Sonnenberg Farm
description: A premier KWPN dressage breeding and training facility on nearly 50 acres on Parrett Mountain in Sherwood, 20 minutes south of Portland. Features a mirrored indoor arena with Premier Equestrian engineered footing, an outdoor arena with Ebb & Flow underground watering, a Euro-Sizer/Walker, round pen, oversized airy stalls, grass fields, and all-weather paddocks. Multiple USDF Horse of the Year and Regional Champion titles. Owned and operated by Dan and Gina Ruediger.

name: Crown Ranch
description: Crown Ranch is a cutting horse facility in Weatherford, Texas, offering boarding and training in the western disciplines. Situated in Parker County's renowned horse country, the ranch provides quality care for performance horses.

name: Tin Top Creek Equine Facility
description: Tin Top Creek Equine Facility is a 100-stall boarding operation six miles south of I-20 on Tin Top Road in Parker County. The facility features three barns including an indoor stall barn, a covered round pen, heated wash bay, private and shared turnout paddocks, and an arena. Full, partial, and self-care boarding options are available.

name: October Hill Farm
description: October Hill Farm is a premier 200-acre equestrian facility in Hudson Oaks, Texas offering hunter/jumper, eventing, and dressage training. The farm features both indoor and outdoor arenas and provides full-service boarding, professional instruction, and horse sales under the guidance of trainer Wendy Gerrish.

trainers.name: Wendy Gerrish
trainers.bio: Professional trainer specializing in hunter/jumper, eventing, and dressage at October Hill Farm.

name: Freeman Ranch & Stables
description: Freeman Ranch & Stables is a horse boarding stable located in Katy, Texas. Situated on a working ranch property in the northwest Katy area, the facility offers boarding services for horse owners in the greater Houston metro region.

name: Bit & Bridle Stables
description: Bit & Bridle Stables is a beloved Hill Country boarding and training facility located in Harper, Texas. With a 4.9-star rating from 88 reviews, the ranch offers privately guided trail rides through the scenic Hill Country landscape, private lessons, equine therapy, and boarding. Run by Tim and Vanessa, the operation is known for its warm, welcoming atmosphere and breathtaking views.

trainers.name: Tim & Vanessa
trainers.bio: Owner-operators of Bit & Bridle Stables, offering guided trail rides, private lessons, and equine therapy in the Texas Hill Country.
